meta.name: FARUMA Master Syllabus
meta.note: Auto-generated from the compiled workbook. Blocks marked missing/stub in the audit report are not yet populated and require extraction from the official NIE PDFs.

Reasoning, Communication and Connection.outcomeStatement: Recognise and use connections among mathematical concepts, between mathematical concepts and other disciplines and communicate mathematical thoughts, situations and methods using relevant vocabulary and tools.

indicators.indicator: Observes patterns, similarities and differences, develop mathematical ideas and make links between ideas.

Structure and Function.outcomeStatement: Observe variety of living things to identify body shape, size and discuss the importance of diversity of living things

indicators.indicator: Identifying that there are different kinds of living things, they differ in body shape, form and have different names and discuss the importance of variety of animals

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Recognise their name, its meaning and how it contributes to their identity.

indicators.indicator: Recognises their name and meaning of their name.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in classroom conversations and discussions in dual language with teacher support using verbal and nonverbal cues.

indicators.indicator: Responds to greetings, and joins in and contributes to shared language experiences such as songs and rhymes.

Demonstrates positive listening behaviour (e.g., look at the speaker; show interest in what the person is saying; focus on key words and phrases; listen without interruption; wait for their turn) ignoring background noise and movement.
Conveys personal information, needs and ideas using learned sentence patterns.
Expresses opinions and preferences using learned sentence patterns.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Listen to oral texts (stories, information and instructions) to make meaning and begin to make inferences.

indicators.indicator: Restates the purpose of a listening activity (e.g., to learn a new fact, to follow an instruction, for enjoyment, etc.).
Follows verbal instructions, suggestions or requests given in a two-step sequence.
Answers Yes/No and WH questions on explicitly stated information (verbally, worksheet, etc.) and begins to make inferences.
Identifies the main ideas or events and retells them in sequence.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Speak in dual language with teacher support to inform, describe, clarify and entertain using 1-3 short sentences and providing some detail.

indicators.indicator: Tells / retells a story, participates in show-and-tell and asks questions, using appropriate words, phrases, facial expressions and gestures.
Gives information about familiar topics (e.g., oneself, family, hobbies, etc.) using dialogue frames or learned sentence patterns.
Uses 1-3 simple statements or utterances to describe familiar pictures and situations.
Recounts a personal experience focusing on a beginning, body and ending, using key words and pictures.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Listen and speak to explore literary texts (themes, characters and plot), make predictions and relate them to personal experiences.

indicators.indicator: Makes predictions about themes, characters and plots using titles, descriptions and illustrations before, during and after listening.
Talks about characters (look, do, like, feel, etc.), and how they relate to personal experience.
Shows understanding of texts by relating cause and effect using because (e.g., Shahida is scared because she does not know how to go home.)
Discusses possible solutions to problems (based on personal experiences) and expresses them through drawings and role-play.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Use polite verbal language and nonverbal cues to speak clearly at an appropriate volume and pace.

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates interaction skills (e.g., turn-taking, maintain eye contact, stay on topic making positive statements) to speak clearly at an appropriate volume and pace.
Begins to use polite social language appropriately (e.g., hello, please, thank you, sorry, excuse me).
Asks questions to clarify meaning or to request for assistance.
Makes connections between classroom discussions and personal experiences.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Give brief oral presentations in dual language with support, using text frames.

indicators.indicator: Engages in informal oral presentations (e.g., show-and-tell, perform action songs, recite a poem, etc.).
Speaks in a meaningful sequence from memory or using simple visual prompts and objects (e.g., retells a story, gives a recount, explains a procedure, recalls key points from non-fiction texts, etc.).
Uses body language, voice (e.g., lower volume in small groups and increase when addressing whole class) and tone (e.g., formal, informal, serious, etc.) to suit purpose and situation.

Reflects on their own speaking with the help of a criteria developed by the class (e.g., tick on a rubric items such as maintains eye contact with the audience, speaks loud enough to be heard, appropriate volume, etc.).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Use skills and strategies of effective listeners to create meaning.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and sets a purpose for listening activities (e.g., to find information, for enjoyment, etc.).
Makes predictions based on prior knowledge and contextual clues (e.g., visuals, setting, and topic) before, during and after listening to oral texts.
Listens to an oral text to complete a task (answer simple questions, recall information, complete a diagram, etc.) using the language expressions and intonations.
Listens to feedback from others and answers questions from the audience.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Identify and manipulate individual sounds of words (onset, rime and syllables) and recognize word classes by function.

indicators.indicator: Identifies similar sounds (e.g., alliterations and rhyme words) and syllables in spoken words.
Blends 3 or 4 phonemes to make a word, and segment words into individual sounds and syllables.
Claps to rhythms in oral texts or repeats a refrain from a poem, or song with an appropriate pattern.
Recognizes nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs and prepositions in spoken language by their function.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Recognize that words have to be ordered in a specific way and begin to accurate short sentences.

indicators.indicator: Produces short statements and asks questions in correct word order (e.g., I have a new bag; Do you like pets? etc.).
Begins to use negation (e.g., I don't like spiders; today is not Friday; It is not red; etc.)
Uses simple and progressive tenses appropriately, although over-generalizations may exist for irregular verbs (e.g., swimmed, keeped).
Produces oral texts of about 1-3 simple sentences on a topic by using simple cohesive devices (e.g., first, second, then, next, after and before) to sequence ideas and events.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in shared and independent reading of texts (e.g., picture books, stories, rhymes, etc.) to make meaning using illustrations, the written text and personal experiences.

indicators.indicator: Reads short familiar texts aloud with some fluency and expression, focusing on key words, phonics knowledge and pictures.
Makes connections between story events and personal experiences during shared and independent reading experiences (e.g., buddy reading, readers' theatre, choral reading).
Retells a familiar story using beginning, middle and end.
Locates some detail in written text, photos, illustrations and other graphics in response to questions or tasks.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Read and view non-fiction texts (information texts, recounts, instructions and procedures) to make meaning using the written words and begin to make inferences.

indicators.indicator: Restates the purpose of a reading/viewing activity (e.g., to learn a new fact, to follow an instruction, for enjoyment, etc.).
Recognizes some basic 'text features' to understand and locate content in information texts (e.g., title, illustration, captions).
Follows basic written directions supported with pictures (e.g., create a simple craft, follow a recipe).
Presents information acquired from texts (ideas, facts, concepts, etc.) through speaking, drawings and other representations.
Answers Yes/No and WH questions on explicitly stated information (verbally and/or in writing to complete a worksheet, etc.) and begins to make inferences.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in reading/viewing a range of texts for pleasure, to improve fluency and to develop a positive attitude towards reading.

indicators.indicator: Selects own books and media texts to read from the library (school/class) or personal collection.
Reads for a sustained period (e.g., 10-15 minutes) independently or with a friend.
Reads and rereads aloud texts modelled by the teacher and receives feedback.
Participate in reading circles to read aloud books and to talk about them.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Explore themes, characters and events to encourage imagination, develop reasoning (e.g., sequence, cause and effect, prediction and character motivation), and instill values.

indicators.indicator: Makes predictions about themes, characters and plots using titles, descriptions and illustrations before, during and after listening.
Discuss texts (characters, their actions and consequences) and begin to make value judgements by relating to personal experiences.
Shows understanding of texts by relating cause and effect using because (Alice is scared because she does not know how to go home.)
Discusses possible solutions to problems and expresses them through drawings and role-play.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Begin to read with understanding by identifying familiar words by sight and unfamiliar ones using graphophonic, semantic and syntax cues.

indicators.indicator: Reads automatically some familiar high-frequency words and words of personal interest with understanding.
Reads unfamiliar words by using grapheme-phoneme correspondence (e.g., sound-letter relationships; blend and segment individual sounds in words; common spelling patterns).
Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using semantic cues (e.g., familiar words, phrases, sentences, and visuals that activate existing knowledge of oral and written language).
Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using syntactic cues (e.g., predictable word order, predictable language patterns and punctuation).
Begins to read shorts phrases and sentences with expression to convey a sense of understanding to the reader (e.g., read a role in a simple readers' theatre script to sound like natural speech).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Practise the skills and strategies of effective readers before, during and after reading (make/confirm predictions, set a purpose for reading, activate prior knowledge, identify textual features and organize information).

indicators.indicator: Predicts content based on images, cover illustrations and titles and reads to confirm predictions.
Identifies and sets purpose for reading activities with teacher support (e.g., to gain information, to answer some questions, for enjoyment).
Builds upon prior knowledge and experiences to make meaning.
Recognizes some basic 'text features' to locate specific content in information texts.
Presents information from texts through discussion, drawing or other representations.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate developing skills and strategies to read, view and comprehend short texts based on familiar topics.

indicators.indicator: Determines question(s) to be answered by reading texts, with teacher support and asks questions at different stages using key words and phrases.
Self-monitors own reading and uses fix-up strategies when meaning is interrupted (e.g., reread, read slowly, read aloud, read on).
Uses key words and phrases to identify the main ideas and supporting details and use graphic organizers to show how they are linked.
Creates a mental image using prior knowledge, experiences and information from the text.
Retells key facts and ideas from shared reading experiences (e.g., story maps, roleplaying).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Recognize common word transformations and language devices used in texts.

indicators.indicator: Reads accurately by applying phonic knowledge and blending sounds in unfamiliar words containing GPCs that have been taught.
Read common exception words, where sounds and spellings do not match (e.g., what, chair, etc.)
Reads poems and rhymes with rhythm.
Recognizes words in written texts by their function and position (e.g., nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs and prepositions).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Derive meaning from texts written using simple sentences for a variety of purposes by focusing on word order and simple cohesive devices.

indicators.indicator: Derives meaning from short statements and questions using word order (e.g. Maniu spoke to his friend. / His father spoke to Maniu; Did Ali give Mariyam a pen? / Did Mariyam give Ali a pen?)
Begins to derive meaning from sentences that use negation (e.g. I don't like spiders; today is not Friday; It is not red; etc.)
Derives meaning from short texts that contain simple and progressive tenses.
Identifies the role of cohesive devices (e.g. first, second, then, next, after and before) in sequencing ideas and events in a short texts on a given topic.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Create personal and imaginative writings and representations for a variety of purposes using a combination of drawings, individual words, short phrases and simple sentences.

indicators.indicator: Joins in teacher modelled writing activities (e.g., write a list (e.g. shopping list, things to do, etc.), caption and a description for a picture, create a class book (names of students and their likes, etc.).
Write messages to express feelings, ideas, likes and dislikes (about a favourite toy, character from a story, etc.).
Recounts personal experiences and events (e.g., 'what I did on Friday').
Writes simple narratives with characters, setting, and a sequence of events based on models.
Contributes to joint construction of poetic forms (e.g., write repetitive simple sentences in teacher guided writing activities).

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Write/create representations of a range of information texts for a variety of purposes using words or sentences, text features and multimodal elements.

indicators.indicator: Writes very simple descriptions of family members, friends, familiar places, etc. using short phrases/sentences, pictures and drawings.
Uses modelled structures to create simple information reports and representations with support from writing scaffolds (e.g., an information report on cats or information posters).
Writes simple explanations with pictures and explain the process or phenomena (e.g., How to make lime juice: Water cycle; etc.).
Creates a writing or representation to persuade an audience (e.g., create a poster on an environmental issue; make an advertisement for a product or event, etc.).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Hold a pencil correctly and with ease to write (using correct letter form and direction; and appropriate spacing of letters in and between words).

indicators.indicator: Sits correctly at a table, holding a pencil easily and correctly (tripod-grasp) to write words, phrases and sentences.
Tilts the page/book in front to facilitate writing.
Writes lower case letters in correct direction, starting and finishing in the right place, with appropriate spacing in and between words.
Writes capital letters in correct form and with a contrast in height to lower case letters.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Begins to practise the process skills of writing to create imaginative and informative texts.

indicators.indicator: Refers to learned knowledge of text structure and grammar to create a new text.
Says aloud what to write and composes sentence orally before writing it.
Expresses the ideas and events using simple sentences.
Checks whether the text makes sense (re-read aloud, read it to another, etc.) and for use of correct spellings and punctuation.
Produces text neatly with illustrations in best handwriting; talks about what the text is about; and shares the writing experience (ideas, thoughts and feelings of what has been learned, etc.).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Spells words correctly and uses them effectively to create an image for the reader.

indicators.indicator: Spells words from memory, taught GPCs and own spelling strategies.
Shows an understanding of word groups and their functions (e.g. determiners, nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions and conjunctions) to compose phrases and sentences.
Uses adjectives and adverbs to purposefully to create an image for the reader.
Uses regular verb forms and some common irregular verb forms of simple and continuous tenses (present, past and future) correctly (e.g., jumped, is/was/are/were jumping and will jump).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Writes simple sentences to demonstrate awareness of word order and some basic punctuation (use of full stops, comma and question marks).

indicators.indicator: Orders words correctly in phrases and simple sentences (including statements and questions).

Uses capital letters, commas, full stops and question marks as below: capital letters to begin a sentence, to write pronoun 'I' and first letter of proper nouns; full stops and question marks to end sentences; commas to separate words in a series.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Creates texts of about 1-3 sentences on familiar topics using appropriate text structures (sequence and description) and features (headings/sub-headings, drawings and bullets/numbering) for effect.

indicators.indicator: Writes 1-3 simple sentences on a given topic.

Sequences ideas by using text structures such as at first, then, next, before and after. Describes a person, place, thing or idea using descriptive features or characteristics (e.g., An ant has six legs; The three bears lived in a small house in a forest., etc.).
Uses headings, subheadings, bullets and numbering for effect.
Uses drawings to illustrate the text.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of some characteristic relating to growth and development; functions of some body parts and the importance of sleep and rest for proper growth.

indicators.indicator: Illustrate various developmental stages, spanning several years of growth and development.
Illustrate healthy choices and behaviors which promote healthy growth and development.
Describe the functions of the organs they learned in foundation.
Identify the five senses and practice positive behavior that protects the senses.
Describe changes that occur as they grow (e.g., loss of milk teeth, height).
Explain why sleep and rest are important for proper growth and good health.

Nutrition.outcomeStatement: Develop an understanding about foods they can eat to enhance their health and practices good hygiene when handling food.

indicators.indicator: Match foods to food groups
Identify healthy and unhealthy foods
Understand the importance of eating breakfast and lunch
Recognize the importance of drinking water and other beverages
Classifies foods according to plant or animal origin
Realise that food gives them energy
Records information about healthy and unhealthy favourite foods.
Identify location of nutrition fact label
Understands that food choices can contribute to a healthy body

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Defines what it means to be healthy.

indicators.indicator: Uses words and pictures to express what it is like to be sad, happy, sick, lonely, scared, energetic or angry and what makes them feel this way
Describes the basic activities that people do to stay healthy and how most of the basic activities are common to all people (sleep, play, balanced diet, exercise, personal hygiene)
Describes any illness he or she, friends or family members have had identify the kind of care (such as emotional support, rest, exercise or medicine they received)

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Understands the components of health-related fitness and interprets information from feedback, evaluation, and self-assessment in order to improve performance.

indicators.indicator: Discuss the presence of germs and where they can be found.
Describe ways to prevent the spread of germs and illness e.g.: wash hands, cover sneeze/cough bathing.
Identify ways to cover sneeze.
Discuss the importance of wearing mask.

Personal Identity.outcomeStatement: Identifies similarities and differences between oneself and others.

indicators.indicator: Describes likes dislike, character, age
Identify characteristics that describe self as special and unique (e.g., physical characteristics, abilities, gender)
Take care of own belongings

Interpersonal skills.outcomeStatement: Develop effective interpersonal skills to maintain relationship, respect and responsibility

indicators.indicator: Describe qualities of a good friend.
Identify ways to get along with peers (e.g. take turn, share materials, show respect).
Identify personal and shared space.
Identify different emotions and feeling that contributes to healthy relationship (e.g. waiting for appropriate time to talk, not interrupting).
Demonstrate age-appropriate ways to resolve conflict with limited assistance.

Resilience.outcomeStatement: Students will develop self-management skills to handle stress and cope with feelings, avoid and seek help in threatening situation

indicators.indicator: Identify personal feelings and behaviors.
Identify coping skills to manage emotions (e.g., count to ten when angry).
Identify trusted adults to notify for help (e.g., school, family, community)
Role play how to tell a parent or trusted adult when feeling upset.

Injury prevention.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of different safety hazards in their surrounding and how to overcome them.

indicators.indicator: Tell/write/sign phone numbers of their parents, guardian, fire, ambulance, police etc.
Identify and obey safety rules at home, school and in the play ground
Discuss safety precautions about bodies of water
Describe safety rules for bus and other vehicles
Discuss the importance of not eating or drinking unknown substances.
Talk about the dangers of talking to a stranger
Discuss harmful effects of bullying

Personal body safety skills.outcomeStatement: Acquire age appropriate personal body safety skills.

indicators.indicator: Identify feelings and physicals signs associated with good bad and confusing touches.
Identify private parts of the body as being parts of the body covered by bathing suits (underwear)
Understands that their bodies belong to them, and that they have the right to say no to anyone who touches them in way that makes them feel uncomfortable
Apply the “No, go and tell rule” in different scenarios

Fundamental Movement Skills.outcomeStatement: Explore different manipulative skills with developmentally appropriate challenges.

indicators.indicator: Bounces a ball continuously using both hands
Throws underarm for accuracy
Throws overarm using preferred and non-preferred hand.
Traps a small rolled ball.
Traps a large ball using hands and feet.
Kicks balls of various shapes and sizes for accuracy.
Strikes with one hand off a tee using a bat or racquet.

Locomotor and Non-locomotor movements.outcomeStatement: Perform different locomotor and non locomotor skills

indicators.indicator: Perform various locomotor skills (run, jog, walk, hop, gallop, jump, skip, crawl, leap)
Perform various non locomotor movement (push, pull, turn, twist, balance, sway)

Swimming and water safety.outcomeStatement: Begins to acquire basic skills associated with swimming and survival techniques

indicators.indicator: Entry into water: Enter and exit water confidently
Sculling and body orientation: Recover from a face down float or glide to a standing or other secure position
Movement and swimming strokes: Move through the water unassisted with feet clear of the bottom for a distance of 3 metres using any propulsion method
Survival skills: Float for 30 seconds holding a rescue floating aid for support and signal for help
Hold an object offered by the rescuer and be pulled to safety
Open eyes and breathe out while submerging the body completely

Health and Skill-related Fitness Activit.outcomeStatement: Participates in physical activities that enhance fitness skill and fitness knowledge

indicators.indicator: Cardiovascular endurance (Skill): Take part at least 3 times a week in moderate to vigorous physical activities that increase breathing and heart rate. (e.g., brisk walking or jogging)
Cardiovascular endurance (Knowledge): Identify that the heart is the most important muscles in the body and increasing the heart rate during physical activities strengthen the heart muscles.
Know that the heart functions as a pump for blood.
Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Skills): Perform a ‘v’ sit for an extended period of time with arms stretched forward.
Moves from a sitting to a standing position and from a lying to a sitting position without using arms.
Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Knowledge): Explain that strong muscles helps prevent injury and produce more force
Flexibility (Skill): Do simple stretching exercises without hyperflexing or hyperextending the joints
Flexibility (Knowledge): Understand that proper body position while stretching will help prevent injury
Body composition (Skill): Engage in uninterrupted movement activities (in games such as tag or follow the leader)
Body composition (Knowledge): Identify body components (e.g., bones, muscles, fats, organs and tissues).
Understand body needs correct portion of food and water to function.
Participate in recognized health fitness assessment which covers cardiovascular endurance, muscular strength and endurance, flexibility and body composition

Reasoning, Communication and Connection.outcomeStatement: Recognise and use connections among mathematical concepts, between mathematical concepts and other disciplines and communicate mathematical thoughts, situations and methods using relevant vocabulary and tools.

indicators.indicator: Uses appropriate terminology to describe, and symbols to represent mathematical ideas.
Checks accuracy of a solution and explains the process using appropriate terminology and symbols and represent, mathematical ideas.
Supports conclusions and explain the process by demonstrating how answers were obtained.
Apply concepts and skills to solve problems posed in mathematics class as well in real life contexts.
Interprets the solution. Does the answer make sense? Is it reasonable?
Counts larger collections by grouping them.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Recognise the value of numbers up to 999.

indicators.indicator: Recognise what each digit represent in a 3 digit number (Eg: use abacus, number cards, number line).
Compare numbers up to 3 digit numbers using related vocabulary and symbols. (Eg: more than, less than).
Partition, 3-digit numbers up to 999 (Eg: 489 = 400 + 80 + 9).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Use ordinal numbers up to 31st in meaningful contexts.

indicators.indicator: Read and use ordinal numbers up to 31st.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Estimate quantities up to 100.

indicators.indicator: Estimate quantities to 100 and use appropriate vocabulary in estimation. Eg: about, not less than, almost ….

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Recognize, copy, continue and create simple patterns (two to four elements).

indicators.indicator: Recognize, copy, continue and create simple patterns (two to four elements) using sounds, actions, shapes, colours, objects and pictures and explain the sorting rule.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore, identify and describe number patterns formed by skip counting forwards or backwards (2's, 5's and 10's).

indicators.indicator: Count forward by 2's, 5's and 10's up to 1000 using a variety of tools and strategies (Eg: move with steps; skip count on a number line; place counters on a hundreds chart; connect cubes to show equal groups; count groups of pennies).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Recognize and use commutative principle.

indicators.indicator: Use the commutative principle (exclude name) in addition. (Eg: 2 + 3 = 5 or 3 + 2 = 5).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Solve problems involving addition (up to 2-digit numerals) with answers to 100 and the corresponding subtraction by using personal strategies for adding and subtracting with and without the support of manipulatives, develop mental strategies and solve 2 step word problems.

indicators.indicator: Solve 2 step word problems involving addition and subtraction.
Develop mental strategies (Eg; counting on, partitioning, rearranging parts) for simple addition and subtraction problems.
Use models/diagrams in solving addition and subtraction problems (Eg; Ten frames, base ten blocks, part part whole method, number line).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Demonstrate and explain the effect of adding zero to or subtracting zero from any number.

indicators.indicator: Add or subtract zero to a number and explain why the product remains same.
Recognize that there are 100 laaris in MVR 1, 200 laaris in MVR 2, ….., …..

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Carry out simple transactions involving money up to 100 and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Pay appropriate amounts and determines appropriate change in shopping, up to MVR 100.
Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of money. (Exclude combinations of MVR and laari in money transactions).
Explore multiplication relating to repeated addition and division relating to repeated subtraction.
Explore that multiplication and division are inverse operations (Eg; Use arrays to describe the relationship between multiplication and division).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of multiplication facts of 2, 5 and 10 and related division facts and solve one step word problems.

indicators.indicator: Identify multiples of 2, 5 and 10 up to tenth multiple using a 100 square grid.
Explore the relationship between multiplication and division facts.
Use of multiplication and division symbol (x and ÷) to write mathematical statements for given situations.
Use multiplication facts of 2,5,10 and related division facts in solving one step word problems (division, without remainder).

Use models/diagrams in solving multiplication and division problems and make multiplication and division stories. Eg: Fathima is 9 years old. Her mother is 4 times as old as her. How old is her mother? (9 x 4 = 36 years old)
Recognizes, read and write fractions as 1/2, 1/4, 1/3, 3/4, 2/3. (Eg: read and write 1/4 as quarter, 2/3 as two thirds).
Represents fractions using concrete materials.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Compare unit fractions using concrete materials and explore that the greater the denominator, the smaller the unit fraction.

indicators.indicator: Uses concrete materials to compare unit fractions with denominators not exceeding 10.
Identifies the reason why the greater the denominator, the smaller the unit fraction (Eg: 2/3 is greater than ¼).
Measure length in cm and m, mass in g and kg, capacity in l.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Describe the importance of using standard units, suggest a suitable unit and equipment to estimate or measures length, mass and capacity.

indicators.indicator: Explain why we use standard units.
Estimate and measure; Length in m/cm, Mass in kg/g, Capacity in l.
Select the appropriate unit and appropriate equipment to measure length, mass and capacity of objects.
Solve simple word problems involving length, mass and capacity.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Use estimation in measuring length, mass and capacity.

indicators.indicator: Estimate length, mass and capacity before measuring and use appropriate vocabulary.
Draw hands on the clock face to show time.
State the five prayer times to the nearest hour or half hour.
Use calendars to locate specific information, such as finding a given date on a calendar.
Recognize, 1 week = 7 days, 1 day = 24 hrs.
Use of abbreviations h and min.
Make models of new shapes by combining cut outs of circle, triangle, square, rectangle, semi-circle and quarter circle.
Copy figures on dot grid or square grid.
Form different models using 3-D shapes such as cubes, cuboids, cones and cylinders.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Recognize, name and make cube, cuboid, cone and cylinder using 2D shapes and explore the difference between 2D and 3D shapes.

indicators.indicator: Identify and name 3D shapes from the environment.
Identify 2D shapes from the surface of 3D shapes and make 3D shapes from 2D shapes (Eg: use a rectangle and 2 circles to make a cylinder).
Identify and describe the difference between 2D and 3D shapes.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Describe the features of 3-dimensional objects (cube, cuboid, cone, cylinder and sphere).

indicators.indicator: Identify, classify and describe the number of flat surfaces, curved surfaces, faces, edges and vertices of three-dimensional objects. Eg; A cylinder has two flat surfaces, one curved surface, no faces, no edges and no vertices.
Draws some land marks when drawing an informal map of own street. (Eg: houses, mosques, shops).
Gives instructions for someone else to follow to find a route through a simple maze drawn on squared paper.
Identifies the 'arms' and 'vertex' of an angle.
Recognises that the length of the arms does not affect the size of the angle.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Recognize and describe turns using the language 'full turn', 'half turn', 'quarter turn', 'three-quarter turn', 'clockwise' and 'anticlockwise'.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes, describes and demonstrates whole, half and quarter turns, both clockwise and anticlockwise.
Collects data using various activities, and make a simple pictogram, where one symbol represents one unit. (Eg: collects data by a show of hands).

Interprets and answers questions based on a set of data. (Eg: Do a simple survey, how many children are in bed before 9, how many children go to bed before 10, are more children in bed by before 9 or before 10?, how many children go to bed after 10?)

Problem Solving and Application.outcomeStatement: Acquire the necessary mathematical concepts and skills to solve problems in mathematics and everyday life.

indicators.indicator: Selects and uses appropriate strategies.

Mathematical Thinking Skills.outcomeStatement: Use mathematical thinking in Mathematics class and real life contexts.

indicators.indicator: Use Mathematical thinking skills such as; classifying, comparing, sequencing, generalizing, analyzing in Mathematics class and real life contexts.

Addition and Subtraction.outcomeStatement: Explore, identify and describe the properties of odd and even numbers (up to 100).

indicators.indicator: Explore the properties of odd and even numbers (up to 100).

Money.outcomeStatement: Describe and identify the values of notes and coins of different denominations up to MVR 100.

indicators.indicator: Identify and orders Maldivian Rufiyaa and laari according to their face value.

Understanding Multiplication and Divisio.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of multiplication relating to repeated addition, division relating to repeated subtraction, recognize the relationship between multiplication and division (multiplication facts of 2,5,10 and related division facts).

indicators.indicator: Use concrete materials and drawings to represent multiplication is combining of equal groups (e.g., use counters to show that 3 groups of 2 is equal to 2 + 2 + 2 and to 3 x 2).

Fractions, Decimals and Percentages.outcomeStatement: Explore, recognize, read and represent 1/2, 1/4, 1/3, 3/4, 2/3 using concrete materials and describe fractions in everyday situations.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes and describe fractions in everyday situations.

Time.outcomeStatement: Use vocabulary related to time; estimate the duration of an event in time units, use calendar to identify dates, read and record time to the nearest quarter hour.

indicators.indicator: Tell time to the quarter hour, using the language of 'past' and 'to'.

Shapes.outcomeStatement: Describe and identify basic shapes and make models of new shapes using cut outs of circle, triangle, rectangle and square.

indicators.indicator: Identify and name basic shapes including semi-circle and quarter circle.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Describe simple directions and draw landmarks of an informal map of a familiar place and describe positions and directions using everyday language.

indicators.indicator: Describe the position of an object in relation to other objects and places.

Angles.outcomeStatement: Identify 'angles' with two arms and vertex in practical situations and recognize that the length of arms does not affect the size of the angle.

indicators.indicator: Identifies angles found in the environment.

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Connect number names, numerals and quantities up to 999 in English, 999 in Dhivehi and write numbers up to 999.

indicators.indicator: Count and writes numbers up to 999 in English and Dhivehi from any starting point.

Length, Mass and Capacity.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of measuring length, mass and capacity by using standard units (cm, m, l, g, kg).

indicators.indicator: Correctly use the abbreviations, cm, m, l, g, kg.

Handling Data.outcomeStatement: Collect, sort and display data in simple forms including representation of data with objects and drawings where one object or drawing represents one data value (pictographs without scale) and interprets the information presented.

indicators.indicator: Collects data and makes a simple table. (Eg: data collected on favourite drinks).

indicators.indicator: Identifying how each of the senses help us to recognize, describe and safely use a variety of materials
Identifying how each of the senses help us to recognize, describe and safely use a variety of materials

Structure and Function.outcomeStatement: Explore that living things grow and change

indicators.indicator: Illustrating the changes and personal development in oneself

indicators.indicator: Recognising inventions that contribute to quality of life such as various equipment, gadgets and machineries used by us.

Resources of the Earth.outcomeStatement: Recognise that living things use the resources of the Earth in a variety of ways

Physical features of Earth.outcomeStatement: Observe the changes that occur in the locality over time

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of the atoll and the island that they belong to

indicators.indicator: Marks the location of the atoll and the island one belongs to on a given map of the Maldives

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Use map skills, (significant map keys, follows directions) to locate and identify important places of their island

indicators.indicator: Identifies and locates the important places of their island (e.g., location of school, mosque, play areas, hospital, parks, island office, police station, etc.)

Society and the Environment.outcomeStatement: Identify and explore the physical (natural) and human geographic features of their environment

indicators.indicator: Uses simple fieldwork and observational skills to study physical and human geographic features of their island

Weather and Climate.outcomeStatement: Explore the local weather conditions and its impact on humans

indicators.indicator: Recognises the two monsoons in the Maldives (South west monsoon, North east monsoon)

Weather and Climate.outcomeStatement: Predict extreme weather conditions in the Maldives and apply safety measures

indicators.indicator: Identifies and interprets some of the extreme weather conditions in the Maldives

Caring for the Environment.outcomeStatement: Becomes aware of important strategies to care for their surroundings/environment (reduce-reuse-recycle)

indicators.indicator: Takes age appropriate actions to practice responsible behaviours in caring for the environment (anti-litter, reduce, reuse, recycle and cleaning campaigns)

Caring for the Environment.outcomeStatement: Gains an understanding of ways in which the environment can be polluted or harmed

indicators.indicator: Recognises different ways that their surrounding environment can be polluted or harmed (e.g., inappropriate disposal of garbage, etc.)

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Value being a member of the social groups that they are a part of

indicators.indicator: Identifies and demonstrates behaviours people practice in order to live together peacefully in groups (e.g., say 'please' and 'thank you', take turns, be considerate of others, help when asked, not laughing at people when they make mistake)

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Identify the social groups that one belongs to and talks about how these groups contribute to an individual's identity (e.g. family, friends, school activity groups)

indicators.indicator: Talks about the social groups that they are a part of (e.g., school, football team, Quran club, etc)

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Value and appreciate ones' own, talents and interests and that of their friends

indicators.indicator: Talks about their self-worth as an individual and shares information about their talents and interests

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Identify important official documents that contribute to one's own unique identity (documents such as passport, National ID card)

indicators.indicator: Recognises and shares information that contributes to their unique identity (such as, filling personal information forms, Personal ID card, Passport and finger print, etc.)

National Identity – We the Maldivians.outcomeStatement: Recognise important national symbols, value and respect the national symbols of the Maldives (emblem)

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes the national emblem/court of arms of the Maldives

Culture and Heritage.outcomeStatement: Value the traditions associated with cultural celebrations practiced in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Engages in activities to value and express love for Maldivian culture (e.g. role-play, read cultural stories, take part in cultural activities, etc.)

Culture and Heritage.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding about the traditions associated with cultural celebrations in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Identifies special events, celebrations, associated with culture (e.g., Fitr and Alha eid, etc.) with the help of different sources such as photographs, stories of grandparents, discussions, drawings, digital technologies, etc.

International Economics and Trade.outcomeStatement: Gains an understanding of imports, exports and about local goods

indicators.indicator: Differentiates between imports and exports

Financial Institutions.outcomeStatement: Becomes aware of the value of money

Financial Institutions.outcomeStatement: Recognise that money is used to buy goods and services

Economic Systems.outcomeStatement: Becomes aware of goods, services and learns about the goods and services used by their family members

indicators.indicator: Identifies 'goods' and 'services'

Economic Systems.outcomeStatement: Explore about age appropriate economical choices they make every day

indicators.indicator: Identify the choices they make every day

Inquiring.outcomeStatement: Begins to locate sources, organize and select information

Inferring and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Begins to process and interpret information and develop critical thinking

Applying and Communicating Findings.outcomeStatement: Begins to apply and share findings with others

indicators.indicator: Identifies ways to share findings with others (e.g., through class displays, assembly items, role-plays, models, pictures, writing)
Begins to reflect on current understanding of a topic (e.g., through pictures, illustrations, etc.)

Time and Change.outcomeStatement: Identify and explore ways in which time and change affect people, places and things

indicators.indicator: Discusses how things change overtime using chronological terms (e.g., 'a long time ago'; 'then and now'; 'now and then'; 'old and new'; etc.)

Roles, Rights and Responsibilities.outcomeStatement: Becomes aware of one's own neighbourhood and gains an understanding of rights, rules and responsibilities to live together peacefully in a neighbourhood

indicators.indicator: Recognises and becomes familiar with places of one's own neighbourhood

Decision Making and Leadership.outcomeStatement: Identify the important leaders in the neighbourhood and their roles

indicators.indicator: Recognises the leaders in a neighbourhood and identifies their roles (police, firefighters, garbage disposal managers, elders, etc.)

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in conversations and discussions (e.g. give/receive information; ask/answer relevant questions) to extend the interaction, using non-verbal cues to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Initiates and responds independently to greetings and contributes to shared language experiences such as songs and rhymes.

Demonstrates positive listening behavior (e.g., pay close attention to the speaker's words; indicate interest and understanding using facial expressions and gestures; comment and ask relevant questions to verify; listen without interruption).
Conveys personal information, needs and ideas providing some detail and elaboration.
Expresses personal preferences and opinions, giving examples.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Listen to oral texts (stories, information and instruction) to make meaning and make inferences.

indicators.indicator: Restates the purpose of a listening activity (e.g., to learn a new fact, to follow an instruction, for enjoyment, etc.).
Follows verbal instructions, suggestions or requests in a two-three step sequence.
Answers questions on predictions, and explicitly stated and inferred information (verbally, worksheets, drawings, etc.).
Identifies the main ideas and events and organizes them sequentially.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Speak for a specific purpose (to inform, describe, clarify, recount and entertain) using 3-5 longer sentences that provide some relevant detail.

indicators.indicator: Identifies purpose for speaking (e.g., tell/retell a story, ask for/ give information, to describe something in detail, etc.).
Uses appropriate language, facial expressions and gestures to suit purpose and enhance meaning.
Exchanges information, ideas and feelings on familiar topics, by asking and answering relevant questions using learned sentence patterns.
Gives presentations in class (e.g., perform action songs and rhymes, report on a known topic, do a roleplay, etc.) using models and drawings.
Recounts a personal experience in a logical sequence that includes who, where, when and what.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Listen and speak to explore literary texts (themes, characters and plot), confirm predictions and relate them to personal experiences.

indicators.indicator: Confirms predictions made on themes, characters and plots, using titles, descriptions and illustrations before, during and after listening.
Talk about characters (looks, do, like, feel, etc.), words and expressions used in the text, relating them to personal experiences.
Shows understanding of texts by relating cause and effect using because (Alice is scared because she does not know how to go home.)
Offers opinions and possible solutions to problems using different strategies (drawings, role-plays, graphic organizers, etc.).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Practise active listening strategies (pay attention, show interest, provide feedback and ask questions) and respond using polite verbal language, intonation, and non-verbal cues.

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates interaction skills (e.g. turn-taking, maintain eye contact, stay on topic, making positive statements, and disagree politely).
Uses polite social language independently (e.g., hello, please, thank you, sorry, excuse me).
Asks questions to clarify, extend meaning or to request information.
Contributes to class discussions by sharing personal experiences (what they have read, heard, or viewed).
Speaks clearly using appropriate volume, tone and pace.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Rehearse and deliver brief formal presentations in class on familiar topics using text frames and appropriate body language to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Gives informal presentations in small and large groups (e.g., perform action songs, recite poems, do a dramatization, etc.).
Speaks in a meaningful sequence from memory or using simple visual prompts and objects (e.g. retells a story, gives a recount, explains a procedure, recalls key points from non-fiction texts, etc.).
Supports presentations with appropriate body language, voice level and tone suit the language purpose and situation.
Peer assess oral presentations using different strategies (e.g., use a criteria, traffic lights - red = don't understand, green = totally get it etc.).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Use skills and strategies of effective listeners to create meaning.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and sets a purpose for listening activities (e.g., to find information, for enjoyment, etc.).
Match predictions based on prior knowledge and contextual clues (e.g., visuals, setting, and topic) before, during and after listening to oral texts.
Listens to an oral text to complete a task (answer simple questions, recall information, complete a diagram, etc.) using the language expressions and intonations.
Reflects upon own listening skills using non-complex rubrics (e.g., makes notes).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Manipulate individual sounds of words, and recognize the structures and patterns of words in oral texts.

indicators.indicator: Identifies sounds of individual words (e.g. onsets, rimes, rhyme words, alliterations) and syllables in spoken words.

Use inflections, affixes and compounding with simple grammatical morphemes to create words that perform different functions (e.g., 'walk', 'walks', 'walking', 'walked', 'walker', 'walking stick', 'stick insect', etc.).
Recites rhymes, poems or riddles focusing on rhythm.
Explore and expand noun phrases (using determiners and adjectives) and verb phrases (giving details such as when, where and how).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Manipulate words to form accurate simple sentences and begin to form compound sentences.

indicators.indicator: Produces longer statements and questions in correct word order and begins to form compound sentences using coordinating conjunctions.
Uses negative forms of simple and progressive tenses correctly (e.g. he doesn't like spiders; today is not Friday; They were not happy; etc.)
Uses simple and progressive tenses appropriately, paying attention to irregular verb forms.
Produces oral texts of about 3-5 simple sentences to inform, describe, recount, recall, etc. on a topic by using simple cohesive devices to link ideas.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in shared and independent reading of texts (e.g. picture books, stories, poems, newspapers, etc.) to make meaning using illustrations, the written text and personal experiences.

indicators.indicator: Reads (aloud) texts with increasing fluency, accuracy, comprehension, expression and a sense of phrasing (e.g., three or more words at a time in meaningful phrases).
Shares personal responses to texts during individual, small group, and whole class reading activities (e.g., silent reading, buddy reading, readers' theatre, choral reading).
Retells a sequence of events from a literary text using beginning, middle, and end.
Demonstrates comprehension by capturing aspects of texts through different strategies (e.g., completing a cloze passage, drawing a story map or acting out the text).

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Read and view non-fiction texts (information texts, recounts, instructions and procedures) to make meaning using the written words and to make inferences.

indicators.indicator: Restates the purpose of a reading/viewing activity (e.g., to learn a new fact, to follow an instruction, to extract specific information, etc.).
Uses some basic 'text features' to understand and locate content in information texts (e.g., steps in a procedure, headings, illustrations, captions).
Follows simple written directions supported with pictures (e.g., create a simple craft, follow a recipe).
Presents information acquired from texts (ideas, facts, concepts, etc.) through speaking, drawings and other representations.
Answers Yes/No and WH questions on explicit and implicit information (verbally, worksheet, etc.).

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Engage in reading/viewing a wide range of texts for pleasure, to improve fluency and to develop a positive attitude towards reading.

indicators.indicator: Selects own books and media texts to read from the library (school/class) or personal collection.
Reads for a sustained period (e.g., 15-20 minutes) independently or with a friend.
Talk about a text of their choice to a small group or whole class and read it or parts of it aloud.
Participate in reading circles to read aloud books and contribute by talking, making sketches, illustrations, role play, etc.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Explore themes, settings, characters and events to encourage imagination, develop reasoning (e.g. sequence, cause and effect, prediction, character motivation and problem-solving), and instill values.

indicators.indicator: Confirms predictions made on themes, characters and plots, using titles, descriptions and illustrations before, during and after listening.
Discuss texts (the author's words and expressions, characters, events, actions and consequences, etc.) to make value judgements by relating to personal experiences.
Shows understanding of texts by relating cause and effect using because (Alice is scared because she does not know how to go home.)
Offers opinions and possible solutions to problems and expresses them through drawings, role-plays, graphic organizers, etc.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Read with understanding using sight words and identifying unfamiliar words quickly using graphophonic, semantic and syntax cues.

indicators.indicator: Reads automatically many high-frequency words, words with common spelling patterns, and words of personal interest with understanding.
Reads unfamiliar words by using grapheme-phoneme correspondence (e.g., letter clusters within words; onset and rime; common spelling patterns; words within words).
Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using semantic cues (e.g., familiar words, phrases, sentences, and visuals that activate existing knowledge of oral and written language).
Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using syntactic (language structure) cues (e.g., word order, language patterns, and punctuation).

Reads short texts with expression to convey a sense of understanding to the reader and an audience (e.g., read aloud to sound like spoken language, making appropriate pauses, stops, and starts indicated by the punctuation).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Practise the skills and strategies of effective readers before, during and after reading (make/confirm predictions, set a purpose for reading, activate prior knowledge, identify textual features and organize information).

indicators.indicator: Predicts content based on information provided by teacher and contextual clues (e.g., title, pictures), and reads to confirm predictions.
Identifies and sets purpose for reading activities with teacher (e.g., to gain information, to answer some questions, to find out how to do something).
Builds upon prior knowledge and experiences (e.g., use KWL charts).
Skims through the text to gain a general impression of the content (e.g., pay attention to pictures, titles, headings, subheadings) and scans to locate specific information.
Contributes to discussions about the information learned from texts and present it through discussion, drawing or other representations.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Use an increasing range of skills and strategies to read, view and comprehend texts on familiar and less familiar topics.

indicators.indicator: Begins to generate question(s) to be answered by reading texts (WH Questions) and pause to ask questions at different stages, using key words and phrases.
Self-monitors own reading and uses fix-up strategies when meaning is interrupted (e.g. reread, read slowly, read aloud, read on, look at visuals and text layout and make connections, etc.).
Identifies the main ideas and supporting details and use graphic organizers to show how they are linked.
Creates a mental image using prior knowledge, experiences and information from the text.
Retells key facts and ideas from shared and individual reading experiences (e.g., story maps, role-playing).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Recognize word transformations and language devices used in texts to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Identifies sounds of individual words (e.g. onsets, rimes, rhyme words, alliterations) and syllables in written texts.

Recognize spellings of words formed by inflections, affixes and compounding with simple grammatical morphemes (e.g., 'walk', 'walks', 'walking', 'walked', 'walker', 'walking stick', 'stick insect', etc.).
Reads rhymes, poems or riddles focusing on rhythm and imagery.
Recognizes noun phrases (using determiners and adjectives) and verb phrases (giving details such as when, where and how) by their position in sentences and their function.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Derive meaning from written texts that uses simple and compound sentences for a variety of purposes by understanding word order and function of cohesive devices.

indicators.indicator: Derives meaning from longer statements and questions using word order and begins to understand meaning from compound sentences.
Derives meaning from negative forms of simple and progressive tenses correctly (e.g. he doesn't like spiders; today is not Friday; they were not happy; etc.)
Derives meaning from written texts that contain regular and irregular verb forms in simple and progressive tenses.
Identifies the role of cohesive devices to link ideas in short texts written for different purposes (e.g. inform, describe, recount, recall, etc.) on a topic.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Create short personal and imaginative writings (3-5 sentences) and representations for a variety of purposes using a combination of drawings, words or sentences.

indicators.indicator: Joins in guided writing activities (to write a message/invitation to a friend/teacher, an excuse note, a reminder of things to do, riddles and jokes, etc.).
Creates writings and representations to express feelings, ideas, likes and dislikes (e.g., create a thank you card for a classmate or family member).
Recounts personal experiences and events (e.g., a visit to the park).
Writes narratives with characters, setting, and a problem.
Contributes to joint construction of short poems (e.g., change certain words from a given poem or rhyme).

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Write / create representations of a wide range of information texts for a variety of purposes using words or sentences, text features and multimodal elements.

indicators.indicator: Writes descriptions of familiar objects, people, things, places, etc. using given frames, headings, pictures and drawings for effect.
Uses modelled structures to create simple information reports and representations with a few logically sequenced ideas (e.g., an information report on bicycles or information posters).
Writes simple explanations / instructions with headings, sub-headings and accompanying illustration to explain a process or phenomena (e.g., How does it rain?).

Creates a writing or representation to state an opinion and gives reason to support the belief (e.g., write a letter to a teacher requesting a specific place to go on a class picnic and include justification).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Hold a pencil correctly to write words, phrases and sentences with increasing speed (using taught phonemes; correct letter form, direction and height; and appropriate spacing of letters in/between words).

indicators.indicator: Sits with correct posture, holding pencil using tripod-grasp to compose texts using letters of consistent size and orientation.
Consistently tilts the page/book in front to write with increasing speed.
Writes lower case letters of the correct size relative to one another, in correct form, direction and spacing in and between words.
Writes capital letters and digits of the correct size, orientation and relationship to one another and to lower case letters.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Engages in the process skills of writing to create imaginative and informative texts.

indicators.indicator: Sequences content according to text structure and uses appropriate tense to create a new text.
Considers what to write before beginning to write (e.g. say out loud what to write; or jot down ideas and key words, etc.).
Writes the ideas and events in the plan using simple and compound sentences to express and combine ideas.
Checks whether the text makes sense (reread aloud, read it to another, etc.), and edit text for grammatical choices, spellings and punctuation to improve meaning.
Rewrites text neatly with illustrations for classroom publication, talks about the experience and shares the writing experience (ideas, thoughts and feelings of what has been learned, etc.).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Spells words correctly and begins to use simple language devices (imagery, alliterations and similes) to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Spells words from memory, taught GPCs, using knowledge of inflections, prefixes and suffixes, referring word lists on display and own strategies.
Shows an understanding of word groups and their functions (e.g. determiners, nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions and conjunctions) to compose phrases and sentences.
Uses adjectives and adverbs, alliterations and similes to enhance meaning.

Uses regular verb forms and common irregular verbs forms of simple and continuous tenses (present, past and future) correctly (e.g., jumped, is/was/are/were jumping and will jump; ate, sang, wrote, etc.).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Write simple and compound sentences to demonstrate awareness of word order and correct punctuation (use of capital letters, full stops, comma, question marks and apostrophe).

indicators.indicator: Orders words correctly in phrases, and simple and compound sentences (including statements and questions).

Uses capital letters, commas, full stops, question marks, exclamation marks and apostrophes as below: capital letters to begin a sentence, to write pronoun 'I' and first letter of proper nouns; full stops, question marks and exclamation marks to end sentences; commas to separate words in a series; apostrophes for common contractions and possessives.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Creates texts using appropriate text structures (sequence, description and compare-contrast) and features (headings/sub-headings, drawings, bullets/numbering, diagrams and charts).

indicators.indicator: Begins to group ideas to write a paragraph of two-three sentences to develop the idea in topic sentence.

Uses the following text structures to organize writing: Sequence (first, second, third, then, next, before and after); Description (who, what, when, where, why or how of a topic/subject); Compare-contrast (similar, same, like and different).
Uses headings/subheadings and bullets/numbering to help a reader to understand the text.
Uses drawings, diagrams and charts to support the text.

Making.outcomeStatement: Describes elements of art (line, colour, shape, form and texture) in nature, in the environment and in artworks.

indicators.indicator: Describes elements of art (lines, shapes, form, colour texture and patterns in artworks and in their environment.

Making.outcomeStatement: Review dots and different kinds of lines and use them to make an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Reviews the different kinds of lines (straight, curved, wavy, zigzag, dotted, spiral, curly).

Making.outcomeStatement: Identify and name warm (red, orange, yellow) and cool (purple, blue, green) colours and use them to create a particular mood in an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and name the warm colours (red, yellow, orange) and cool colours (purple, blue, green).

Making.outcomeStatement: Identify horizontal, vertical, diagonal, thick, thin, short and long lines and use them to make an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Explores horizontal (asleep), vertical (standing up) and diagonal (falling), thick, thin, long, short lines in artworks (e.g., Piet Mondrian), and in structures such as buildings and bridges. 30

Making.outcomeStatement: Identify and use organic shapes to make an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies organic shapes in artworks (e.g., collage by Henri Matisse), in objects in nature and the environment.

Making.outcomeStatement: Identify a variety of forms (3D shapes) and use them in art making.

indicators.indicator: Identifies basic forms (3D shapes) such as cone, cube, sphere and cylinder. 31

Making.outcomeStatement: Mix white to a colour to create a lighter colour (e.g., light green) and black to a colour to create a darker tone (e.g., dark green) and use them to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies lightness and darkness of a colour (e.g., light blue/dark blue).

Contributes to class discussions by sharing experiences of self and that of others (from real life, books, movies, etc.).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Rehearse and deliver short formal presentations on familiar topics using visual aids and appropriate body language to enhance meaning.

Reflects upon own listening skills using non-complex rubrics (e.g., makes notes).

Answers WH questions on explicit and implicit information (verbally, worksheet, etc.).

indicators.indicator: Selects own books and media texts to read from the library (school/class) or personal collection.
Reads silently for a sustained period (e.g., 20-30 minutes) independently or with a friend.
Talk about a text of their choice to a small group or whole class and read it or parts of it aloud.
Participate in reading circles to read aloud books and contribute by talking, making sketches, illustrations, role play, etc.

indicators.indicator: Confirms predictions made on themes, characters and plots, using titles, descriptions and illustrations before, during and after listening.

Shows understanding of texts by relating cause and effect using because (Alice is scared because she does not know how to go home.)
Offers opinions and possible solutions to problems and expresses them through drawings, role-plays, graphic organizers, etc.
