Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using semantic (meaning) cues (e.g., prefixes, suffixes, base words, phrases, sentences, and visuals that activate existing knowledge of oral and written language).
Predicts the meaning of unfamiliar words using syntactic (language structure) cues (e.g., word order, language patterns, punctuation).
Reads with expression to convey a sense of understanding to the reader and an audience (e.g., read a dialogue or a poem for two voices with a partner, using appropriate phrasing and expression).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Practise the skills and strategies of effective readers before, during and after reading (make/confirm predictions, set a purpose for reading, activate prior knowledge, identify textual features and organize information).

indicators.indicator: Predicts content based on information provided by teacher and contextual clues (e.g., title, pictures), and reads to confirm predictions.
Identifies and sets purpose for reading activities independently (e.g., to gain information, to find out a specific information).
Builds upon prior knowledge and experiences (e.g., uses a preview guide).
Participates in discussions about the purpose and the likely audience of texts (e.g., fairy tales, stories, advertisements, cartoons).
Shares information learned from texts and organize it logically using different representations (e.g., writes information under categories provided by teacher - size, colour).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Use an increasing range of skills, strategies and background knowledge to read, view and comprehend longer texts on familiar and less familiar topics.

indicators.indicator: Generates question(s) to be answered by reading texts independently (WH Questions, making connection questions) and pause to ask questions at different stages, using key words and phrases.
Self-monitors own reading and uses fix-up strategies when meaning is interrupted (e.g. reread, read slowly, read aloud, read on, look at visuals and text layout and make connections, etc.).
Identifies the main ideas and supporting details (with attempts at paraphrasing) and use graphic organizers to show how they are linked.
Creates a mental image using prior knowledge, experiences and information from the text.
Retells key facts and ideas from shared and individual reading experiences (e.g., story maps, role-playing).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Recognize word transformations and language devices of increasing sophistication used in texts to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Identifies sounds of individual words (e.g. onsets, rimes, rhyme words, alliterations) and syllables in written texts.

Recognize spellings of words formed by inflections, affixes and compounding with simple grammatical morphemes (e.g., 'walk', 'walks', 'walking', 'walked', 'walker', 'walking stick', 'stick insect', etc.).
Reads stories, dialogues and poetry in different voices and tones and begins to talk about imagery (descriptive words, similes and onomatopoeia) and rhythm.
Recognizes noun phrases (using determiners and adjectives) and verb phrases (giving details such as when, where and how) by their position in sentences and their function.

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Derive meaning from texts written using simple, compound and complex sentences for a variety of purposes by understanding word order and function of cohesive devices.

indicators.indicator: Derives meaning from simple and compound sentences using word order and begins to understand meaning from complex sentences.
Derives meaning from negative forms of simple and progressive tenses correctly (e.g. he doesn't like spiders; today is not Friday; they were not happy; etc.)
Derives meaning from written texts that contain regular and irregular verb forms in simple, progressive and present perfect tenses appropriately.
Identifies the role of cohesive devices to link ideas in short texts written for different purposes (e.g. inform, describe, recount, recall, etc.) on a topic.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Create personal and imaginative writings (6-10 sentences) and representations for a variety of purposes and audiences using a combination of drawings, words or sentences.

indicators.indicator: Joins in guided and shared writing activities (to write a message or invitation to a friend/teacher, an excuse note, a reminder of things to do, riddles and jokes, diary entry to share personal thoughts, etc.).
Creates writings and representations to express feelings, ideas, likes and dislikes (e.g., writes a reading response, letter to a friend).
Recounts personal experiences and events (e.g., my birthday party).
Writes narratives with characters, setting and a sequence of events, a complication and resolution.
Begins to experiment with poetic forms (e.g., changes certain words from a given poem or rhyme, use imagery, i.e. words and phrases that appeal to the senses) to write short poems.

Communicative Purpose.outcomeStatement: Write and create representations of a wide range of information texts for a variety of purposes and audiences using words or sentences, text features and multimodal elements.

indicators.indicator: Writes descriptions of people, things and places (e.g., teacher, Islamic Centre, a bicycle) for a specific audience (classmates, parents, teacher, etc.).
Creates simple information reports and representations with a few logically sequenced ideas and some subject specific vocabulary (e.g., information posters or information reports on bats).
Writes simple explanations / instructions with headings, sub-headings, accompanying illustration to explain a process or phenomena (e.g., How does it rain?).

Creates a writing or representation to state an opinion and gives reason to support the belief (e.g., write a letter to a teacher requesting a specific place to go on a class picnic and include justification).

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Hold a pencil correctly to write words, phrases and sentences with increasing speed (using taught phonemes; correct letter form, direction and height; appropriate spacing of letters in and between words) and begins to join letters.

indicators.indicator: Sits with correct posture, holding pencil using tripod-grasp to compose texts using letters of consistent size and orientation.
Consistently tilts the page/book in front to write with increasing speed; begins using some diagonal and horizontal strokes to join adjacent letters.
Writes lower case letters of the correct size relative to one another, in correct form, direction and spacing in and between words.
Writes capital letters and digits of the correct size, orientation and relationship to one another and to lower case letters.

Skills and Strategies.outcomeStatement: Engages in the process skills of writing to create imaginative and informative texts for a specific audience.

indicators.indicator: Selects appropriate text structure for a writing purpose and sequences content for clarity and audience impact.
Considers what to write before beginning to write (e.g. say out loud what to write; or jot down ideas and key words, and putting each idea into a different sentence).
Writes the ideas and events using simple and compound sentences; begins to use complex sentences to express and combine ideas.
Checks whether the text makes sense (reread aloud, read it to another, etc.) and edit texts for grammatical choices, spellings and punctuation to improve meaning.
Rewrites text neatly with illustrations for classroom publication, talks about the experience and shares the writing experience (ideas, thoughts and feelings of what has been learned, etc.).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Spell words correctly and begin to use language devices (imagery, alliterations, similes and onomatopoeia) to enhance meaning.

indicators.indicator: Spells words from memory, taught GPCs, common exception words, using knowledge of inflections, prefixes and suffixes, referring word lists/dictionaries and own strategies.
Shows an understanding of word groups and their functions (e.g. determiners, nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions and conjunctions) to compose sentences.
Uses adjectives and adverbs, alliterations, similes and onomatopoeia to enhance meaning.
Uses regular/irregular verb forms of simple and continuous tenses (present, past and future) correctly (e.g., jumped, is/was/are/were jumping and will jump; ate, sang, wrote, etc.).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Write simple and compound sentences with ease and begins to write complex sentences to demonstrate awareness of word order and use of correct punctuation.

indicators.indicator: Writes simple and compound sentences (including statements and questions) in correct word order and begins to write complex sentences.

Uses capital letters, commas, full stops, question marks, exclamation marks, apostrophes and quotation marks as below: capital letters to begin a sentence, to write pronoun 'I' and first letter of proper nouns; full stops, question marks and exclamation marks to end sentences; commas to separate words into a series, dates, addresses and locations; apostrophes for common contractions and possessives; quotation marks (often inconsistently).

Language Structures and Features.outcomeStatement: Creates texts of about 6-10 sentences on familiar topics, using appropriate text structures (sequence, description, compare-contrast, cause-effect, and problem-solution) and features (headings/subheading, drawings, bullets/numbering, diagrams and timelines).

indicators.indicator: Adopts paragraph structures to write a topic sentence and two or more sentences to develop the main idea.

Uses the following text structures to organize writing: Sequence (first, second, third, then, next, before, after, and finally); Description (who, what, when, where, why or how of a topic/subject); Compare-contrast (similar, same, like, unlike, and different); Cause-effect (since, because, if, causes and therefore); Problem-solution (problem, since, as a result, and idea).

Describes similar and contrasting features or characteristics (e.g., Ants and butterflies are insects. They both have six legs. But butterflies are different. They have beautiful colorful wings; etc.).
Uses heading/subheadings and bullets/numbering to help a reader understand the text.
Uses drawings, diagrams, charts and timelines to support the text.

Making.outcomeStatement: Compare the visual qualities (art elements and principles) in artworks and in their immediate environment.

indicators.indicator: Compares the line, shape, colour, texture, form, pattern in two different artworks and/or two objects they see in their everyday life.
Asks questions and gather information (e.g. make sketches) to make meaning about what they see.
Explores and record ideas for art making in a personal sketchbook.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Make an artwork of a building using different kinds of lines.

indicators.indicator: Reviews different kinds of lines (horizontal, vertical, diagonal, curved, straight, zigzag, wavy, spiral).
Create an artwork of a building using different kinds of lines.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore organic lines (wavy, curved, swirly, spiral) in artworks and in the environment and use them to make an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and name organic lines (e.g., wavy, curved, swirly, spiral) in artworks or in the environment.
Creates an artwork using organic lines.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore monochromatic colours and use monochromatic colours to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies monochromatic colours in artworks.
Creates an artwork using monochromatic colours (e.g., tints and shades of blue).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Create at least two tints (mixing white to a colour) and at least two shades (mixing black to a colour) and use them to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Mixes white to a colour to create at least two tints.
Mixes black to a colour to create at least two shades.
Creates a painting using a range of tints and shades.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore organic shapes in artworks and use a combination of organic shapes and lines to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies organic shapes in artworks (e.g., Bop by Elizabeth Murray) and in objects seen every day (e.g., paint blob, shape of splash).
Creates an artwork using organic shapes and lines (e.g., wavy, curved, swirly, spiral).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Examine positive and negative shapes/space in artworks and use these concepts to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies positive and negative shape/space in artworks.
Creates an artwork using positive and negative shape/space.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Examine form combinations (e.g., sculptural, architectural) and create sculptures by combining forms that make up the various parts of the sculpture.

indicators.indicator: Looks at and identifies how form combinations are used in sculptural and architectural works.
Creates a sculpture (form) of their favourite animal by combining different parts of the animal using papier-mache technique.
Creates action figure sculptures by combining different parts of the body.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify and create visual textures and use them to create an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies visual textures in artworks (e.g., artwork by Eric Carle) and in other images.
Uses lines, shapes and dots to create textures (e.g., draw texture/patterns on the papier mache sculpture).
Creates visual textures on paper using a variety of methods (e.g., wash and salt, crush paper printing) and use them to make a collage.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Create patterns by reflecting, rotating, scaling and/or stretching shapes.

indicators.indicator: Identifies four ways that can be used to create patterns.
Creates a pattern by rotating, reflecting, scaling and stretching shapes and uses it to decorate an artwork.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Make an artwork to show variations (e.g., variation in sizes, colour, texture and/or patterns).

indicators.indicator: Observes and discover how artists have used variation in size, colour, shape, pattern, texture in their artworks.
Creates a digital artwork to show variation in letters size, colour, fonts to tell a story about someone.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Examine the center of interest as emphasis in artworks and create a focal point in an artwork.

indicators.indicator: Identifies the use of focal point (centre of interest/emphasis) in an artwork.
Investigates ways to create a focal point in an artwork.
Creates a focal point in an artwork.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Make visual artworks and designs based on their own ideas, experiences, and imagination.

indicators.indicator: Creates artwork about self, family or friends that focuses more on details such as facial features (eyes, smiles), skin colour, hairstyles, body height, weight.
Creates artwork based on significant life events and personal experiences.
Creates an artwork about a range of themes/topics such as personal and social interest and issues in the community (e.g., ‘Save the turtles’).
Makes an artwork based on their imagination.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Create artworks based on the observation of objects, emphasizing value changes.

indicators.indicator: Makes detailed drawings from observation (e.g., using a magnifying glass observe and draw the patterns formed by veins on leaves).
Observes and draw simple objects or things from the environment and add details.
Uses various wet and dry media to draw on different surfaces.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore various tools, types of paints (e.g., water colour, poster colour) and techniques to create artworks.

indicators.indicator: Paints with different consistencies such as such as transparent and thin paint or opaque and thick paint (e.g., paint some areas of a still life with opaque/thick paint and other areas with thin/transparent washes).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Develop skills by using different media together to create artworks.

indicators.indicator: Creates a pattern using different media (e.g., use paint and then when dry use crayons/pens to highlight or outline).
Experiments with a variety of colour media together and their effects (e.g., use paint with other materials such as oil or/and salt).
Creates a collage with a wide range of natural and/or processed materials.
Compares the qualities and properties of materials such as paint, clay and found objects and selects appropriate applications to represent something or someone they like.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explore and use printmaking techniques to create artworks.

indicators.indicator: Makes a transfer monoprint (e.g., draw a self-portrait on a piece of foam board and make a print of it; cut and arranges cardboard shapes (positive and negative), apply paint/ink and take a print of it).
Makes stamp prints (e.g., crushed paper printing) and use the textured paper to make an artwork/object.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Practice using fabric and fibres to make artworks.

indicators.indicator: Creates a simple carboard loom for weaving.
Makes a woven fabric collage using a cardboard loom.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Develop skills in using a variety of natural and/or processed materials to express ideas in 3D.

indicators.indicator: Uses papier-mâché technique to create your favourite animal.
Uses aluminium foil and/or pipe cleaners to create a human figure in action.
Make an assemblage sculpture of something using found objects.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Develop skills in using digital media to create artworks for a particular purpose.

indicators.indicator: Uses digital media as a tool in art making (e.g., take photos of objects they inspire in the nature or artworks in their community using a digital camera/mobile phone/ipad).
Explores simple techniques using computer-generated programs (e.g., PowerPoint/Word/digital apps) to create artworks.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Follow instructions and use art media safely and responsibly.

indicators.indicator: Uses art media and equipment safely.
Uses art materials sensibly and appropriately (e.g., follow instructions when using and cleaning art materials so that they are not wasted).
Demonstrates appropriate ways to organize and care for the materials (e.g., cover the tables with newspaper before starting work).
Demonstrates understanding that each art form has a way of being presented (e.g., window mounts for prints and drawing).
Makes a decision about how to display their artwork (e.g., a sculpture usually needs to be walked around to see it from different points of view).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Describe their own and the works of others using appropriate vocabulary and art terminology.

indicators.indicator: Reflects on preferences about their artwork in order to improve it.
Discusses their own and the work of others using appropriate vocabulary and art terminology (e.g., art elements).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Evaluate their own artwork and works of others using simple strategies.

indicators.indicator: Evaluates artworks they view and make using simple reflective tools.
Demonstrates appropriate behaviour when receiving comments about their own artworks (e.g., Listens carefully to others’ feedback/comments without offence).
Uses bar lines as a division for beats.
Uses terminology and notation for half note and whole note.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify the difference between changing tempos.

indicators.indicator: Identifies the difference between changing tempos (fast and slow) rhythmic and melodic patterns.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify pitch (repetition, unison, small range of pitch patterns based on the pentatonic scale).

indicators.indicator: Identifies the difference between high and low sounds, same, different, repeated, small range of pitch patterns based on the pentatonic scale.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify and use dynamics (getting louder, getting softer, very soft (pp) and very loud (ff)).

indicators.indicator: Recognizes the difference between loud and soft sounds, getting louder, getting softer, very soft (pp) and very loud (ff).
Selects appropriate levels of loud and soft in performing.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify introduction, verse, chorus rounds and ostinato (form).

indicators.indicator: Identifies introduction, verse, chorus rounds and ostinato.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify timbre (sound qualities of instruments; matching different sounds to specific instruments).

indicators.indicator: Identifies sound qualities of different instruments.
Identifies the most common instruments in each of the four families by name, picture, and sound.
Explores, classifies and matches different sounds to specific instruments.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Identify texture (melody and accompaniment).

indicators.indicator: Identifies whether a melody is harmonized or unharmonized.
Identifies the difference between melody and harmony (accompaniment).

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Sing expressively with accurate rhythm, pitch, appropriate tempo, dynamics, articulation and phrasing.

indicators.indicator: Sings expressively, showing awareness of the meaning of the text.
Sings with accurate, clarity and reasonable technique, a variety of songs (e.g., folksongs) in 2/4, 3/4, 4/4 and 6/8 time.
Sings expressively and with appropriate tempo, dynamics, articulation and phrasing.
Sings as an ensemble, melodic ostinato and 2-3 part canons with increasing rhythmic and melodic complexity.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Develop performance skills in playing classroom instruments with correct timing, individually and in groups.

indicators.indicator: Performs individually and as an ensemble, rhythmic, melodic and harmonic patterns in 2/4, 3/4, 4/4 and 6/8 time.
Describes the way which sound is produced on various instruments.
Creates short rhythmic and melodic phrases in question-and-answer form.
Composes and performs 2-part rhythmic phrases of at least 4 bars.
Uses graphic notations to represent sound.
Creates soundscapes or melodic compositions using the basic techniques of selecting loops.
Demonstrates appropriate audience behavior when responding during and after a performance.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Sing and listen to music from diverse cultures and compares music from various cultures.

indicators.indicator: Sings memorized songs from diverse cultures.
Describes places and occasions where different types of music are experienced and performed.
Identifies differences and commonalities in music from various cultures.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Explain specific elements of music and how they communicate mood and meaning.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes and explains the effects of different musical choices using music terminology (e.g., slow music that is loud can be dramatic or ceremonial whereas slow music that is soft can suggest thoughtfulness).
Takes on different roles in a drama (e.g., taking on the point of view of a fictional character; listening and responding in role; adopting a role and maintaining focus).
Establishes and sustains a fictional setting in a drama performance.
Explores different character traits and develop relationships between characters.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Perform dramas that communicate ideas based on important personal events, fictional stories and events and issues in their community.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and uses pantomime, tableau and improvisation to retell a part of Maldivian history (e.g., November 3 event) and important personal events.
Retells and act out songs, stories, or fictional stories from different cultures.

Identifies and uses pantomime, tableau and improvisation to portray environmental or issues in the community (e.g., saving trees, caring for animal, bullying) and based on moral values (e.g., empathy, respecting others).
Uses props, objects, and/or images and background music to enhance the drama.
Performs skills and audience awareness when performing dramas.
Demonstrates appropriate behavior (e.g., paying attention) as an audience, when viewing drama.

(unspecified).outcomeStatement: Make responses that involve identifying and reflecting on the use of the elements of drama, using drama terminology.

indicators.indicator: Makes responses that involve identifying and reflecting on the use of the elements of drama, using drama terminology.
Writes about and discusses with others the meaning and intended purposes of their own drama using drama terminology.
Responds to a performance by communicating ideas about it, particularly regarding technical aspects of drama, such as sets and costumes.

Responding.outcomeStatement: Display and present their artworks, intentions and making process with others.

indicators.indicator: Displays and presents their own artwork to a specific audience through informal discussion, classroom display and school exhibition.

Responding.outcomeStatement: Create music and practises their own and others’ music to perform for a specific audience.

indicators.indicator: Improvises with voice and classroom instruments, pentatonic melodic and rhythmic responses.

Responding.outcomeStatement: Perform dramas to a range of audiences.

indicators.indicator: Performs and presents dramatic scenes from a story to a range of audiences.

Making.outcomeStatement: Explore rhythm (experience and identify time signatures 2/4, 3/4, 4/4; use bar lines as a division for beats; terminology and notation for half note, whole note).

indicators.indicator: Identifies time signatures 2/4, 3/4, 4/4.

Making.outcomeStatement: Explore the five (5) elements of drama (voice, movement, role and situation) to create dramas.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and use voice (loud, soft, varying loud and soft; pitch variation; pace; volume) and facial expressions and gestures to create belief in character and situation when performing drama.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the developmental milestones; positive and negative self-image; healthful choice regarding digestive system

indicators.indicator: Construct a timeline of own life showing development from birth to present (developmental milestones).
Identify positive and negative influence related to body image
Identify healthful choices that promotes healthy digestive system / Identify basic structures and functions of the skeletal system.
Illustrate the important roles of the heart and lungs
Describe how choices regarding sleep, nutrition, and health care practices impact overall health
Demonstrate how to communicate with parents or trusted adults about growth and development

Nutrition.outcomeStatement: Develop and understanding about foods they can eat to enhance their health and practices good hygiene when handling food.

indicators.indicator: List six nutrients
Name some energy giving food, body building food and protective and regulatory food
Talk about the importance of a balance meal
Identify the impact of high sugar and high caffeine drinks
Explain the importance of choosing healthy beverages
Plans and makes a healthy sandwich and applies safe food handling practices.
Identify the types of information on nutrition fact label
Identify food that are high in sodium and sugar
Describe benefits of limiting consumption of sodium and added sugars.

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Discuss ways in which the local community contributes to the health of individuals and groups.

indicators.indicator: Identifies health concerns in the community incorporating different dimension of health.
Identifies some of the services the community provides to promote the health of groups and individuals and identifies how they are used
Describes the special health needs of different groups in the community and how these are met.

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Identify communicable diseases prevalent in the Maldives and their preventive measures.

indicators.indicator: Define common communicable diseases (e.g., common cold, flu, measles).
Identify how communicable diseases spread.
Identify types of parasites that infect the body (e.g., lice, ticks, hookworm).

Personal Identity.outcomeStatement: Identifies groups and places that are part of one’s life.

indicators.indicator: Describe their various group identities.
Understands that everyone has multiple identities
Recognize positive attributes of self, family, and classmates (e.g., physical characteristics, abilities, qualities)
Understands needs, wants and likes may differ from each other

Interpersonal skill.outcomeStatement: Develop effective interpersonal skills to maintain relationship, respect and responsibility.

indicators.indicator: List characteristics and benefits of healthy and unhealthy relationship.
Identify situations in which friends may be helpful.
Describes ways to express feelings so as to avoid conflict.
Describe the effects of conflict on relationships.

Resilience.outcomeStatement: Students will develop self-management skills to handle stress and cope with feelings, avoid and seek help in threatening situation.

indicators.indicator: Identify unhealthy behaviors that express certain emotions
Identify personal stressors.
Demonstrate ways to manage different kinds of stress (e.g., praying, reciting Quran, role play, writing a journal)

Injury prevention.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of different safety hazards in their surrounding and how to overcome them

indicators.indicator: Identify the danger of playing with electricity.
Demonstrate respect for the laws and regulation regarding safety.
Identify steps for home fire prevention and their home fire escapes
Demonstrate stop, drop and roll when clothing is on fire
Understand that individuals are responsible for their own personal safety
Tell a trusted adult if been asked to dare
State reasons why it is unsafe to take dares.

Personal body safety skills.outcomeStatement: Acquire personal body safety skills and decision-making skills that can be used in times of crisis to promote safe living.

indicators.indicator: Recall and discuss when to use personal body safety rules.
Define strangers and identify ways to protect themselves from strangers.
Decide between safe and dangerous situation that occurs in various environments

Fundamental Movement Skill.outcomeStatement: Perform a range of manipulative skills in response to stimuli, including equipment, words and signals

indicators.indicator: Throws underarm and overarm for accuracy and distance using preferred and non-preferred hand.
Catches using preferred hand.
Traps balls of different sizes and shapes using preferred and non-preferred hand and foot.
Kicks using either foot.
Strikes with both forehand and backhand
Performs simple routines using a short rope (e.g. side swings and jumps, alternate foot and basic steps).
Passes to a stationary target (e.g., passes with hand, foot or equipment).
Dribbles with preferred foot/hand.

Locomotor and Non-locomotor movements.outcomeStatement: Perform locomotor skills in game situation with increasing agility

indicators.indicator: Perform locomotor skills with increasing agility
Perform locomotor skill in a variety of games

Swimming and water safety.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate basic swimming ability and survival techniques

indicators.indicator: Entry into water: Perform a step in entry
Sculling and body orientation: Demonstrate sculling head first
Movement and swimming strokes: Swim 50 metres using action which resembles two or more strokes (15 metres using an above-water arm recovery stroke and 15 metres using an underwater arm recovery)
Butterfly: Perform dolphin kick for a distance of 5 metres
Survival skills: Demonstrate swim for 1 minute holding a rescue floatation aid
Using a rigid object pull a partner to safety
Underwater skills: Perform a surface dive, swim underwater and recover an object from water of chest depth

Health and skill related fitness activit.outcomeStatement: Participates in physical activities that enhance fitness and fitness knowledge

indicators.indicator: Cardiovascular Endurance (Skill): Take part at least 3 times a week in moderate to vigorous physical activities that increase breathing and heart rate (e.g., active games involving running and chasing such as football, handball, netball)
Cardiovascular Endurance (Knowledge): Locate areas on the body where a pulse can be found (e.g., wrist, chest, neck)
Compare changes in heart rate before, during, and after physical activities
Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Skills): Perform abdominal sit up, push up, forward and side lunges and squats to improve endurance and increase muscle strength.
Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Knowledge): Identify how to lift heavy objects properly without injuring back
Flexibility (Skill): Hold stretching of hips, shoulders, hamstring, triceps, biceps, quadriceps, back and neck without injury.
Flexibility (Knowledge): Identify and locate abdominals, calf muscles and gluteus muscles.
Body composition (Skill): Engage in uninterrupted moderate to vigorous physical activities (in games such as tag or follow the leader, active games involving running and chasing)
Body composition (Knowledge): Understand that body mass can be measured using Body Mass Index (BMI).
Understand that calories determine the amount of energy they can expend for various physical activity.
Participate in recognized health fitness assessment which covers cardiovascular endurance, muscular strength and endurance, flexibility and body composition

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Recognize and write Roman numerals up to 12 and use Roman numerals in necessary situations.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes and writes Roman numerals from zero to twelve.

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Recognize Arabic Indic (Hindu Arabic) numerals, including zero up to 30.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes Arabic Indic numbers from zero to thirty.

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Recognize everyday situations that use integers (eg; temperature), the location of negative whole numbers in relation to zero and place them on a number line.

indicators.indicator: Locates and represents integers on a number line and orders integers.

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Recognize, read, represent, write and order up to 6-digit (5 digit & 6 digit) numbers in English and Dhivehi.

indicators.indicator: Identifies the patterns of number names and writes numbers in figures and words in English and Dhivehi.

Number Concept.outcomeStatement: Make estimations and round numbers to a specified place value and use the appropriate vocabulary of estimation.

indicators.indicator: Estimates numbers and explains how the estimation is done.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Explore and recognize decimal number bonds of 1.

indicators.indicator: Uses pictorial representations to find the decimal number bonds to 1.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Express the amount of money using decimal notations and record remainders (division) as fractions and decimals.

indicators.indicator: Writes the amount of money using fractions and decimal notations (e.g., MVR 4.25).

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Perform addition and subtraction of fractions (include mixed fractions) with same denominators and simplify the fractions.

indicators.indicator: Identifies mixed numbers and explains that mixed numbers are greater than 1.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Calculate fractions of a quantity and apply the concept of calculating fractions of a quantity in solving problems related to real life and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Calculates fractions of a quantity using concrete materials, diagrams or models.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Recognise that the place value system can be extended to tenths and hundredths and make connections between fractions and decimal notation.

indicators.indicator: Explores decimal place value, recognises and applies decimal notation to express tenths and hundredths as decimals (e.g., 0.2 is the same as 2/10).

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Represent decimals on number line and compare decimals up to 2 decimal places.

indicators.indicator: Compares and orders decimals on number line.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Recognise and use diagrams to show families of common equivalent fractions (denominators 2–12) and explain the role of the numerator and denominator.

indicators.indicator: Uses strip diagrams, number lines or other models to investigate and record equivalent fractions.

Fractions and Decimals.outcomeStatement: Represent and compare two unit fractions and two like fractions (fractions with the same denominator) and explain the role of numerators and denominators in comparing fractions.

indicators.indicator: Compares fractions in terms of size and places fractions in order on a number line.

Four Operations.outcomeStatement: Divide up to 3 digits by 1 digit and 4 digits by 1 digit (with remainders) using personal strategies and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Uses written strategies for division and explains the process (e.g., long division method).

Four Operations.outcomeStatement: Multiply up to 4 digits by 1 digit, 2 digits by 2 digits and 3 digits by 2 digits using personal strategies and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Uses mental and written strategies for multiplication and explains the process (e.g., area model, splitting strategy).

Four Operations.outcomeStatement: Solve problems involving addition (up to 4-digit numerals) and the corresponding subtraction by using personal strategies and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Uses mental and written strategies for addition and subtraction and explains the process.

Four Operations.outcomeStatement: Apply the relationships of odd or even number pairs established to check the accuracy of calculations and use estimation and rounding to check the reasonableness of answers to calculations.

indicators.indicator: Predicts whether the answer to a calculation will be even or odd by using the properties of the numbers.

Four Operations.outcomeStatement: Explore number patterns resulting from performing multiplication and division and create and describe patterns.

indicators.indicator: Explores and describes number patterns resulting from performing multiplication.

Handling Data.outcomeStatement: Gather and organize data, display data in line graphs and interpret the results and solve the related problems.

indicators.indicator: Recognises that line graphs are used to represent data that shows continuous change.

Handling Data.outcomeStatement: Gather and organize data, display data in bar charts and interpret the results and solve the related problems.

indicators.indicator: Collects data by conducting a simple survey about general issues in the school or community and displays data.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Use simple maps and grids to represent position and follow routes, using compass directions.

indicators.indicator: Uses NE, SE, SW and NW to indicate north-east, south-east, south-west and north-west on a compass rose.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Identify and name types of angles (exclude reflex), estimate, measure, compare using degrees and draw angles using a protractor.

indicators.indicator: Identifies types of angles in the environment.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Connect decimal representations to the metric system.

indicators.indicator: Expresses measurements using decimal notations (e.g., 150 cm = 1.50 m).

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Estimate lengths, masses, capacity, area, perimeter and time using appropriate units and explain the estimation process.

indicators.indicator: Develops a sense of 1 km, 1 ml, 1 minute by relating to familiar everyday examples.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Selects and uses the appropriate unit and tools and measures perimeters of basic shapes.

indicators.indicator: Investigates and recognises that shapes with the same perimeter may have different dimensions.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Measures, records, compares and estimates areas and perimeters using cm and m.

indicators.indicator: Recognises the need for standard units as a formal unit to measure area.

Time.outcomeStatement: Apply 24-hour time and am and pm notation in real-life situations, convert them and construct timelines.

indicators.indicator: Compares 12- and 24-hour time systems and converts between them.

Time.outcomeStatement: Explore the relationship between units of time. Carry out conversions of hours to minutes to seconds and vice versa (multiples of 60), solve problems related to time and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and uses the correct operation for converting units of time.

Shapes.outcomeStatement: Compare and describe features of quadrilaterals and create models using different shapes.

indicators.indicator: Identifies right angles in quadrilaterals.

Shapes.outcomeStatement: Identify and describe the line symmetry of triangles and quadrilaterals.

indicators.indicator: Identifies line symmetry by cutting, folding and turning shapes and justifies that the figure is symmetric about the line of symmetry.

Problem Solving and Application.outcomeStatement: Analyse, plan, carry out the plan and reflect on the process in problem solving situations.

indicators.indicator: Analyses and selects the most suitable problem-solving strategies in solving problems in the contexts of Mathematics and real life.

Reasoning, Communication and Connection.outcomeStatement: Recognise and use connections among mathematical procedures and mathematical concepts, between mathematical concepts and other disciplines and provide valid reasons by communicating mathematical thoughts using relevant vocabulary and tools.

indicators.indicator: Uses appropriate terminology to carry out mathematical conversations and symbols to represent mathematical procedures and concepts.

Mathematical Thinking Skills.outcomeStatement: Use mathematical thinking to solve non-routine problems systematically and efficiently in Mathematics class and real-life contexts.

indicators.indicator: Uses mathematical thinking to solve challenging or non-routine problems that are beyond the daily routine questions.

Science as a Human Endeavour.outcomeStatement: Recognise that science involves making predictions, identifying and describing patterns

indicators.indicator: Discussing to recognise ways in which scientists gather evidence for their ideas and develop explanations.

Science as a Human Endeavour.outcomeStatement: Explore the impact of scientific knowledge on their daily life and actions

indicators.indicator: Investigating a range of jobs related to use of materials and identifying how science knowledge is used.

Design and Making.outcomeStatement: Discuss ideas and present the design idea, select materials to make a product and evaluate the product

indicators.indicator: Discussing and collecting ideas for making designed solutions.

Evaluating and Communicating.outcomeStatement: Reflect on procedures and results obtained in an investigation and share observations and results

indicators.indicator: Discussing the procedure and results to identify what went well and ways to improve the investigation.

Formulating Questions and Making Predict.outcomeStatement: With guidance formulate questions relating to familiar contexts that can be investigated and make predictions

indicators.indicator: Asking questions to identify problems based on prior experiences and with guidance formulating questions that can be tested.

Planning and Conducting Investigations.outcomeStatement: Plan and carry out scientific investigations with guidance, considering fair testing and safe use of equipment and materials

indicators.indicator: Discussing and planning with guidance to identify procedures that needs to be followed while conducting an investigation considering ways to make it a fair test.

Recording and Interpreting Data.outcomeStatement: Describe observations, results and identify patterns/trends or relationships in data

indicators.indicator: Discussing predictions observations/results and compare with predictions, and discuss possible reasons for predictions being correct/incorrect.

Recording and Interpreting Data.outcomeStatement: Use a range of methods to record observations/data including charts, tables and drawings and make precise measurements using standard units

indicators.indicator: Discussing and suggesting observations that could be made to collect data and/or information about the questions and predictions.

Force and Motion.outcomeStatement: Explore and investigate the effect of forces (contact and non-contact) on objects

indicators.indicator: Describing friction as a force that impedes motion and generates heat.

Energy.outcomeStatement: Explore that variety of sources of heat exist and heat can be transferred from one object to another

indicators.indicator: Identifying different sources of heat and describe how heat can be produced.

Structure and Function.outcomeStatement: Observe and identify the life processes in living things

indicators.indicator: Observing to identify the characteristics of life as 'Movement, Reproduction, Sensitivity, Growth, Respiration, Excretion and Nutrition'.

Structure and Function.outcomeStatement: Observe that living things have life cycles and have offspring similar to themselves

indicators.indicator: Observing to identify that all Living things have offspring's similar to parents and are usually with two parents.

Interaction.outcomeStatement: Investigate how plants and animals depend on each other, the habitat and the environment

indicators.indicator: Investigating the populations of plants and animals that exist in various habitats (e.g., reef, desert, seashore, river, tropical forest and tundra).

Physical Features of Earth.outcomeStatement: Observe to identify that weather changes occur overtime

indicators.indicator: Describing the role of the sun in the Earth's weather systems.

Resources of the Earth.outcomeStatement: Explore that air and water are essential components of the Earth system

indicators.indicator: Exploring the major parts, or components, of the Earth system and make connections among those components.

Resources of the Earth.outcomeStatement: Explore and investigate about ways communities and individuals can protect the Earth's resources and environment

indicators.indicator: Describing ways in which living things, including humans, depend on air and water.

Materials and their Properties.outcomeStatement: Explore the properties of natural and processed materials and identify that certain physical properties are linked to their use

indicators.indicator: Observing to identify the link between the property of materials and their use.

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of one's own identity, positive character traits and values oneself and others for their unique character traits.

indicators.indicator: Becomes aware that having an identity is a basic human right.

Makes artworks inspired by different artworks/artists from different times and cultures, including local artists.
