Shapes.outcomeStatement: Identify and describe the line and rotational symmetry of polygons.

indicators.indicator: Determines lines (axes) of symmetry and the order of rotational symmetry of polygons, including the special quadrilaterals.

Shapes.outcomeStatement: Investigate, make, draw and identify the nets that can be used to create the prisms; cubes, cuboids, pyramids, cylinders and cones.

indicators.indicator: Distinguishes between (flat) nets which are two-dimensional and objects created from nets which are three-dimensional.

Time.outcomeStatement: Solve problems involving converting between units of time, organize personal time, interpret timetables, manage scheduled activities and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Solves problems related to time conversion.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Measure and construct angles and shapes of specified degrees using a protractor and ruler, and classify them as acute, right, obtuse, reflex or straight angles.

indicators.indicator: Measures angles using a protractor and names them.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Use angle properties of triangles to find unknown angles and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Investigates and discovers that the angle sum of any triangle is 180°.

Positions and Directions.outcomeStatement: Use angle properties of quadrilaterals to find unknown angles and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Investigates and discovers that the angle sum of any quadrilateral is 360°.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Solve problems involving volumes and capacities of triangular and rectangular prisms and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Builds triangular prisms (3D figures) with unit cubes and finds the volume in cubic units.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Solve problems related to the volumes of irregular solids using a displacement strategy and explain the process.

indicators.indicator: Explains why and where we use the displacement strategy.

Measurement.outcomeStatement: Estimate lengths, masses, capacity, area, perimeter and time using appropriate units and explain the estimation process.

indicators.indicator: Estimates lengths, masses, capacity, area and perimeter of a given object, figure or a place.

Chance.outcomeStatement: Conduct simple chance experiments, identify and describe possible outcomes.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and discusses the variation in results of repeated trials of simple chance experiments (e.g., flipping a coin).

Handling Data.outcomeStatement: Represent, read, and interpret data using simple pie charts and understand the appropriate use of these charts in visual data presentation.

indicators.indicator: Calculates the fraction and/or percentage represented by each sector of a pie chart given the underlying raw data.

Handling Data.outcomeStatement: Conduct simple surveys on school or community issues, display the data using appropriate graphs; bar charts, line graphs and Venn diagrams, and solve problems based on the data.

indicators.indicator: Collects data by conducting a simple survey about environment issues and displays data using appropriate scales and graphs.

Structure and Function.outcomeStatement: Observe and identify cells as the basic unit of life, and compare the basic structures of plant cells and animal cells

indicators.indicator: Identify cell as the smallest unit of structure and function in all living things and state that all living organisms (plants, animals, and microorganisms) are made of cells

Identities and Social Groups.outcomeStatement: Identify and explain self-identity by recognising personal strengths, setting goals for self-improvement, and building healthy self-esteem.

indicators.indicator: Explains what self-identity means and describes how personal traits, values, and experiences contribute to shaping one's identity.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate refusal skill to protect boundaries; express respectful communication; understand the importance of hygiene practices during adolescence; explain the importance of multiple dimensions of health and identify the relationship between cells, tissues, and organs in forming systems.

indicators.indicator: Healthy body awareness: Demonstrate refusal skills to protect personal boundaries and to avoid or reduce health risks.
Healthy body awareness: Use healthy and respectful words and actions to express friendship, attraction, and affection.

Healthy body awareness: Explain the importance of regular hygiene practices during adolescent years (i.e., effective washing helps to control acne and body odour, use of deodorant helps to control body odour, dental hygiene for maintenance, appearance and fresh breath).
Dimensions of health: Explain the interrelationship of the multiple dimensions of health (physical, mental, emotional, social and spiritual health).
Health: Identify the relationship between organs, tissues, and cells that form body system.

Nutrition.outcomeStatement: Students will be able to describe the benefits of healthy food and beverage choices, how nutrition and physical activity impacts personal health, and demonstrate personal efficacy in improving their health.

indicators.indicator: Nutrients: Describe the importance of micronutrients.
Nutrients: Create a meal plan for a week using recommended portion and serving sizes.
Hygiene and food preservation: Discuss different ways of food preservation.
Calories: Define and interpret a percentage daily value.
Calories: Use nutrition labels to determine and compare the healthiness of different food.
Calories: Discuss the disadvantages of consuming too much sugar/salt.
Goal setting: Assess progress toward achieving a healthy eating goal.

Common Disease & Disease Prevention.outcomeStatement: Students shall understand components related to disease prevention and exhibit behaviors to promote health.

indicators.indicator: Communicable disease / Prevention: Determine how hereditary factors and health behaviors impact health.

Medicine: Identify common misuses of nonprescription and prescription drugs: using another person's prescription medication; combining drugs for another effect; over-medicating; not following prescription instructions.
Decision Making: Predict the positive and negative consequences of a decision.

Self concept.outcomeStatement: Analyzes the impact of media, including social media, on the formation of self-identity, develops strategies for responsible and healthy media engagement, and is motivated to set personal goals that are congruent with their values and aspirations.

indicators.indicator: Media literacy & self-identity: Analyze the influence of media, including social media, on self-identity.
Media literacy & self-identity: Discuss responsible and healthy ways to engage with media.
Media literacy & self-identity: Encourage goal-setting exercises that align with personal values and aspiration.

Interpersonal skill.outcomeStatement: Apply conflict resolution strategies to resolve interpersonal conflicts peacefully.

indicators.indicator: Conflict resolution: Uses conflict resolution strategies, such as compromise and active listening.
Conflict resolution: Practice resolving conflicts through roleplays and real-life scenarios.
Conflict resolution: Promote the importance of peaceful resolutions and avoiding aggressive behaviour.

Resilience.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate effective problem-solving and decision-making skills in fostering resilient relationship.

indicators.indicator: Resilience in relationship: Understanding the role of social support in building resilience.
Resilience in relationship: Exploring the importance of empathy and kindness in fostering resilient relationships.

Injury prevention.outcomeStatement: Student will understand and learn specific behaviors that promote injury prevention and personal safety.

indicators.indicator: Determine the severity of an injury or emergency and act accordingly.
Interpret emergency action plans in the event of a natural disaster at home, at school, or in the community (storms, tsunamis etc.).
List the safety precautions that could be taken at home, in school and in the neighborhood (for example during PE period, staying home alone, when using internet).
Internet safety: Recognizing and avoiding online scams and fraudulent website.
Internet safety: Discuss about online gaming and setting appropriate screen time limits.

Personal body safety skills.outcomeStatement: Students will recognize and practice health-enhancing behaviors to reduce health risks.

indicators.indicator: Violence and bullying: Identify situations that threaten personal safety and may result in abuse: physical, social, mental, sexual.
Violence and bullying: Identify various behaviors associated with abuse, bullying, violence, and injury (e.g., physical, verbal, cyber, emotional).

Fundamental Movement Skills.outcomeStatement: Perform a broad range of fundamental movement skills and demonstrate how the body can move in relation to other objects and people.

indicators.indicator: Demonstrate refined manipulative movements skills in games and sports.
Throw: Throw an object with increased velocity and accuracy.
Catch: Catch an object with increased velocity and accuracy.
Kick: Uses a variety of kicks with defenders and show increased velocity and accuracy.
Hit: Step towards and strike a moving object showing controlled pattern.
Dribble: Uses feet to dribble with control and agility at a greater speed.

Swimming.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate confidence in swimming 50 meters, using a recognized swimming stroke.

indicators.indicator: Entry into water: Demonstrate a stride entry.
Sculling and body orientation: Perform a backward and a forward somersault in the water.
Sculling and body orientation: Perform an egg beater kick.
Movement and swimming strokes: Swim continuously 25m freestyle, 25m survival backstroke or side stroke, 25m back crawl, 25m breaststroke.
Butterfly: Swim butterfly for 15 meters using an efficient stroke and correct breathing technique.
Survival skills: Enter deep water using feet first entry.
Survival skills: Submerge feet first, swim underwater on the back looking up at the surface.
Survival skills: Swim 50 meters quickly as if escaping from a dangerous situation.
Underwater skill: Scull, float or tread water for 3 minutes waving for help intermittently.

Health and Skill Related Fitness Activit.outcomeStatement: Perform physical activities that enhance fitness and fitness knowledge.

indicators.indicator: Cardiovascular endurance (Skill): Participate 2-3 days each week, in continuous moderate to vigorous physical activity to improve cardiovascular endurance.
Cardiovascular endurance (Knowledge): Describe the role of physical activity in maintaining good health.
Cardiovascular endurance (Knowledge): Identify activities that cause the heart rate to increase.

Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Skill): Perform abdominal sit up, push up, forward and side lunges and squats to improve endurance and increase muscle strength (this could be achieved by performing different types of animal walks, wheel barrows, and games such as tug of war).
Muscular Endurance/Muscular Strength (Knowledge): Identify the activities that can be accomplished more efficiently with stronger muscles.
Flexibility (Skill): Perform stretching of hamstring, biceps, triceps and quadriceps without injury.
Flexibility (Knowledge): Identify the muscles being stretched during a stretching session.
Flexibility (Knowledge): Identify and locate hamstring, biceps, triceps and quadriceps.
Body composition (Skill): Engage in uninterrupted movement activities (in games such as tag or follow the leader, active games involving running and chasing).
Body composition (Knowledge): Identify how to maintain healthy body composition.
Body composition (Knowledge): Understand the relationship between energy in and energy out.
Fitness outcomes: Participate in recognized health fitness assessment which includes cardiovascular endurance, muscular strength and endurance, flexibility and body composition.

Educational Gymnastics.outcomeStatement: Refine coordinated actions, developing self-awareness and skills that facilitate ongoing participation in educational gymnastics.

indicators.indicator: Static Balance: Perform balance shapes on beam or bench (e.g. knee scale, lunge, arabesque, v-sit).
Landing: Perform landing on hands-falling forward and landing on hands-falling backwards.
Locomotion: Perform cartwheels from or over object (e.g., bench, ball).
Spring: Runs to bench or beat board and lands on mat.

Number Types (N1).outcomeStatement: Understand the concept of numbers, identify and apply the knowledge of different types of numbers.

indicators.indicator: Recognize the natural numbers as counting numbers, whole numbers and integers.

Belief Systems.outcomeStatement: Identify Islamic followers who were blessed with glad tidings of paradise

indicators.indicator: Recognises and appreciates the great 10 followers who were given glad tidings of paradise

Belief Systems.outcomeStatement: Identify the spread of Islamic civilisation and the contributions to Islam by significant Islamic leaders

indicators.indicator: Recognises and describes significant leaders and famous people of the Islamic civilizations and their contributions

Belief Systems.outcomeStatement: Recognise and appreciate the leadership role of Mohamed (SAW) in spreading Islam across the world

indicators.indicator: Explains the story of Muhammad SAW, the beginning of revelation and the important events that led to the spread of Islam across the countries due to his hard work

Historical Inquiry Skills.outcomeStatement: Apply appropriate terms, skills and data to interpret, sequence of historical events and time periods

indicators.indicator: Explains why and how historians use eras and periods to construct, organize and explain human activities overtime

Historical Inquiry Skills.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the process and nature of historical inquiry

indicators.indicator: Becomes aware and comprehends the nature of history and archaeology in understanding the past

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Discover and find out about the early societies and their way of life

indicators.indicator: Shows understanding about hunting and gathering as a way of life and its implications

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Explore and investigate about folklores, myths associated with some of the islands and atolls of Maldives and investigates about the patterns recognisable in the names of different islands and atolls

indicators.indicator: Gathers information about the folklores and myths that are associated with some of the atolls and islands of the Maldives

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Find out and depict understanding about events/stories that describe suffering among groups in the local communities

indicators.indicator: Identifies and recognises the time period of tsunami

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Investigates about the situation and circumstances of the Maldives during world war 1

indicators.indicator: Interprets the situation and condition of the Maldives during world war 1

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Recognize and demonstrate understanding of ideas and events that bought significant changes to the world in the 19th century

indicators.indicator: Describes causes and events that gave rise to World War I

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Explore and describe the past political turmoil, changes and events that became turning points in the political arena of Maldives after the 1900's

indicators.indicator: Identifies, recognises and describes the political Developments in Maldives after the 1900's

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness about the different colonial powers in the Maldives and how Maldives gained independence

indicators.indicator: Explores the events and the situation of Maldives during the rule of Portuguese

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of imperialism and the colonial rule in South Asia and South East Asia

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates understanding of the events and ideas behind imperialism and colonialism in the South and South East Asia

Change Over Time.outcomeStatement: Identify and gain understanding of how early settlements began and explore the events/stories that are related to the beginning of village life

indicators.indicator: Explores the idea of the transition from hunter gatherer to settled life

Change Over Time.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding about the regime of sultanates and dynasties in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes the beginning of sultans and the formation of dynasties in the Maldives

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Recognize the geographical features in the different regions of the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes the process of the formation of atolls

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Explore and gain knowledge about the processes and patterns in the physical environment

indicators.indicator: Describes the physical processes that affect the natural environments of regions

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Discuss about the internal structure of the Earth, process and formation of lithosphere and rocks

indicators.indicator: Describes the internal structure of the Earth

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness about natural hazards, the nature and interactions of the environment that causes natural hazards

indicators.indicator: Explores the reasons and causes that cause natural hazards that affect people's lives

Geography, A Sense of the World.outcomeStatement: Recognize the place, role and branches of the concept of geography

indicators.indicator: Discusses and explains the meaning of geography, branches of geography

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Apply map reading skills to interpret maps

indicators.indicator: Interprets and discuss various types of maps: physical, political, topographic, thematic and etc.

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Explore the various geographical tools, map skills to interpret space and location

indicators.indicator: Explains the geographical tools that can be used to explore an environment – maps, thermometers, graphs, statistics, photographs, compasses, models and fieldwork

Human Systems and Interactions.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness and understanding of sustainability and about the approaches to retain sustainability for future purposes

indicators.indicator: Explores some issues in the local environment that needs to be sustained and conserved

Human Systems and Interactions.outcomeStatement: Explore about some ways of how people interact with the environment, use its resources and factors that affects the interactions of resources

indicators.indicator: Describe and identify agriculture as a system

Human Systems and Interactions.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of settlement, and describe a region as an area of settlement related to geographic characteristics

indicators.indicator: Explains the terms settlement, site, situation, pattern and shape of settlement

Features of Worldwide Destinations.outcomeStatement: Explore the nature of tourist destinations

indicators.indicator: Recognizes and describes the nature of tourist destinations

Introduction to Travel and Tourism.outcomeStatement: Explore and demonstrate awareness about the concept of travel and tourism

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates awareness and describes the concept of travel and tourism

Impacts of Tourism.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding about the different impacts of tourism

indicators.indicator: Identifies the different types of tourism impacts

Tourism in the Maldives.outcomeStatement: Explore and demonstrate awareness about tourism in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Explores the concept of tourism in the Maldives

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore the idea of sports in a cultural context and how sports and culture are inter-related

indicators.indicator: Recognises how culture can be spread and known through sport

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Recognize and demonstrate understanding about the richness and uniqueness of the Giraavaru culture

indicators.indicator: Explores ideas, dressing and other features of the Giraavaru culture that makes them unique and distinct

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore features that are inter-related to culture and identity

indicators.indicator: Describes some of the major components of culture and the common characteristics across cultures

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Investigate and show appreciation about different aspects of cultural eras in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Describes and use cultural aspects to study an era and a region of the Maldives (based on clothing, social institutions, food, infrastructure, way of life)

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Identify gender roles in cultural celebrations in the past (Distant past)

indicators.indicator: Recognises and highlights the roles of different genders in relation to the cultural celebrations carried out across different areas of Maldives

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore aspects of Maldivian culture that reflects change and continuity

indicators.indicator: Recognises and discusses that some cultural practices stay the same and some change overtime

Heritage.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of local heritage and the importance associated with the heritage of a country

indicators.indicator: Recognises, values and appreciates the tangible and intangible heritages found in Maldives that are of significance

Rights, Responsibilities and Citizenship.outcomeStatement: Explore the concepts of rights, duties, responsibilities, (human rights, fundamental rights and the rights of the child) and civic rights and obligations

indicators.indicator: Explores the idea of what is meant by human rights and the concept of fundamental rights

Rights, Responsibilities and Citizenship.outcomeStatement: Identify, when and how national symbols came into being, its role in building citizenship and its progress at various stage

indicators.indicator: Identifies and recognizes the national symbols and its role in building citizenship and love for one's own nation

Government Systems.outcomeStatement: Explore the idea of different types of government and the impacts of systems of governance on people's lives

indicators.indicator: Recognizes and identifies the concept of governance

Political Parties.outcomeStatement: Recognise and demonstrate awareness about party systems and the inter relation of democracy and party system

indicators.indicator: Explains the concept of political parties

Structure and Function of the Government.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the electoral process in the Maldives and the structure and functions of the government of Maldives

indicators.indicator: Explains the basic purpose of government in the Maldives

Law and Order.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the term law, the law making process and the importance and law and order in the country

indicators.indicator: Describes law and the process by which law comes into existence

Law and Order.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness and understanding about the significance and the role of the constitution of Maldives

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates an understanding about the constitution of Maldives and explain its role

Social Systems and Social Virtues.outcomeStatement: Explore about some situations where fundamental rights are deprived and why some of the fundamental rights are not accessible to all

indicators.indicator: Recognises that some people do not have access to some of the fundamental rights

Social Systems and Social Virtues.outcomeStatement: Investigate about the different contexts and ways that one can act socially responsible for the common benefit of people and communities to which one belongs

indicators.indicator: Identifies positive ways in which as individuals one can promote and work for the rights of the people

Applying and Communicating.outcomeStatement: Apply and communicate findings by reflecting on, applying and sharing information with an audience

indicators.indicator: Explains that findings from investigations can lead to the need for further investigations

Processing and Translating.outcomeStatement: Identifies how to process and translate information and develop critical thinking

indicators.indicator: Creates a plan of action to address the chosen problem or issue

Planning.outcomeStatement: Become aware on how to prepare for an investigation

indicators.indicator: Reflects on current understandings of a topic (e.g., using graphic organisers for brainstorming, concept maps, cluster web)

Research.outcomeStatement: Identify, recognise sources, organise and select relevant information

indicators.indicator: Uses different parts of a book (e.g., table of contents, indexes, glossaries, appendices, bibliographies) in locating and judging appropriateness of information

Regional and Voluntary Organisations.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness of organisations that Maldives is a part of

indicators.indicator: Locates and identifies the regional and global organisations that Maldives is a part of

Regional and Voluntary Organisations.outcomeStatement: Recognise the role and developmental work of NGOs in the local community and explore possibilities for character building to work in NGOs

indicators.indicator: Identifies some NGOs in the Maldives and describe their work in the local community

Globalisation.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the relationship of global communities, the role of technology in the globalized world

indicators.indicator: Reflects on the idea of what globalization means and what it means to be a part of the global world

Globalisation.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding and awareness of the positive and drawbacks of being a part of the globalised world

indicators.indicator: Recognises the Benefits/consequences of global interdependence (social, political, economic)

International Relations and Internationa.outcomeStatement: Show awareness and knowledge about the international relation, international organizations, the underlying factors that led to the formation of the international organisations and their roles and functions

indicators.indicator: Identifies with the idea of international relations and the role of international and regional organisations

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply the elements of drama in various genres and styles of theatre.

indicators.indicator: Uses different styles of drama in classroom presentations.
Applies characterisations, conflicts motivation and settings in the drama.
Integrates the 5 W's (who, what, where, when, why).
Sets the stage according to the plan (blocking concepts) they make.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply process and skill in acting to perform monologues and during informal class presentations.

indicators.indicator: Conveys characterisation by using effective voice modulation techniques.
Portrays character using appropriate acting skills such as proper facial expressions, voice modulations, gestures, etc.
Plays a character in a drama with confidence.
Discusses the importance of using appropriate props, sets, make-up, costumes, sounds and lighting in a production to portray characters.
Develops tension and suspense in a drama using character and actions.
Uses classroom space as a way of staging the presentation (plays/drama).
Writes a script for a given story in groups using plot, tension, climax and resolution and follows through to production.
Discusses the technological means that can be used in presentations such as the effect of using background music.
Works in groups to enhance creative ideas in planning a theatrical production.

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Reflect, describe, analyse and communicate feelings, ideas and understanding in response to their own theatrical work and the work of others.

indicators.indicator: Discusses performance and presentation experiences to identify what has been effective.
Expresses their own views of drama elements and issues.
Identifies and uses basic drama vocabulary.
Evaluates a drama presentation after watching it using a scale.

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Discuss the understanding of a variety of drama and theatre forms, traditions and styles from the past and present, and their socio-cultural and historical contexts.

indicators.indicator: Recognises varying cultural perspectives and how they are expressed through drama.
Identifies some of the varied roles of theatre in different communities and cultures.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Create and improvise vocal melodies and vocal accompaniments using a variety of vocal techniques.

indicators.indicator: Sings the notes of given chords with accurate pitch.
Identifies intervals by ear (unison, major third, perfect fifth and octave).
Identifies their own vocal range, uses appropriate techniques over chest voice, head voice and falsetto.
Performs a two parts vocal composition in groups.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Understand and perform the use of scale in instruments and play traditional beats.

indicators.indicator: Performs accurately and independently, alone and in small ensembles, with good posture, good playing position and good breath.
Performs at least three major scales and arpeggios as well as a chromatic scale with any instrument of their own choice (one octave). (Recommended: recorder, keyboard, guitar)
Understands five styles of boduberu beats and performs at least three basic beats.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Compose and arrange music within guidelines specified by the teacher.

indicators.indicator: Finds notes of major scales when the root is given.
Composes and arranges a three parts (bass, tenor and alto) vocal composition within a specified guideline.
Creates melodic call and response in various time signatures.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Read, write and perform rhythms and melodies as found in notated music.

indicators.indicator: Reads and interprets standard rhythmic notation in 2/4, 2/2 and 6/8 time.
Reads and writes syncopated rhythms.
Identifies multi-measure rests.
Identifies various repeat signs (section repeats, previous bar repeats, D.S and D.C).
Identifies keys by the key signature.
Notates rhythmic patterns dictated by the teacher.
Notates simple melodic patterns dictated by the teacher.
Transposes notes in octaves.

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Apply critical thinking strategies to analyse, reflect on and respond to a variety of music and musical experiences.

indicators.indicator: Expresses analytical and/or personal responses to musical performances.

Uses musical terminology to analyse ways in which the elements are used in the music that they perform, listen to and create (for example listen to a folk song or a current popular song and describe how the use of various elements affect their response to the music).

Identifies and gives examples of their strengths and areas of improvement as composers, musical performers, interpreters, and audience members (for example keep a practice journal, record and analyse their performances throughout the term).

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Investigate different technologies behind musical instruments and explore various styles of traditional music.

indicators.indicator: Analyses some historical, cultural, and technological influences on style, genre and innovation in music (for example the impact of invention of the piano or the electric guitar).
Explores various styles of cultural music activities (boduberu, thaara, bandiya and dhandijehun) with regards to the time signature, tempo, variations, forms and mood.
Performs various styles of cultural songs.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Use the elements of art in artworks to communicate ideas, messages and understanding for a specific audience and purpose.

indicators.indicator: Line: Uses lines expressively in two- and three-dimensional artworks (for example look at Arabic calligraphic lines and use a similar style for a design based on letters of your name, using brush and ink).
Line: Uses diagonal and converging lines to create depth of space (for example create a drawing of an interior of a room or the exterior of a building using one-point and two-point perspectives).

Shape and form: Uses icons and symbols in artworks to communicate an idea or a message (for example a painting that uses symbols to represent conflict and resolution; a design of a letter of the alphabet using shapes, symbols, colour and font style to represent a selected animal and its habitat).
Shape and form: Creates a new logo design for a Maldivian resort/boat company, based on designs of shapes and colours of the beach environment.
Shape and form: Creates a variety of artworks using shapes and forms (both geometric and organic).
Colour: Identifies and mixes complementary colours.
Colour: Identifies hue and colour intensity (for example dullness or neutralising, colour intensity by mixing with its complementary hue).
Colour: Creates multimedia artworks with some understanding of different colour models such as RGB and CMYK, and web safe colours.
Texture: Creates a variety of visual and actual textures using a variety of tools, materials and techniques (for example use of texture in a landscape work).

Value: Demonstrates the use of shading techniques (for example hatching, cross-hatching, stippling) to create five levels of value in an artwork to show the illusion of 3-D form (for example use values to create an inked still life).
Space: Uses informal converging lines to create the illusion of depth/space.
Space: Uses blue or complementary colours in shadows and shading to create depth, one- and two-point perspectives.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of composition using multiple principles of design and the 'rules of thirds' to create narrative artworks and/or artworks on a theme or topic.

indicators.indicator: Creates a pattern using repetition of shapes, values, colours, textures and lines (for example a gift wrapping paper, fabric, phone case etc).

Creates an artwork to show implied action/movement (for example the 'freeze frame' effect of an object in motion, such as a bouncing ball suspended in mid-air or a runner about to take the next step).
Creates art using standard proportion (for example an oil pastel drawing of peers in sports or dance using correct human proportion).
Demonstrates the use of symmetrical balance in an artwork (for example Islamic stained glass patterns, 'The Flower Seller' by Diego Rivera).
Creates an artwork to show contrast in colour, value, textures, shapes and/or lines (for example a portrait showing two different feelings/emotions).
Creates an artwork to show a particular area of focus, and the rules of thirds (for example a DVD cover design or a movie poster to communicate information about the content).

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Create images and objects in response to ideas, feelings, observation, memory, imagination and issues from a variety of point of views.

indicators.indicator: Uses a visual journal to document the process of the development of ideas (for example keep a visual diary that describes and shows all the stages of a work's development).

Demonstrates a variety of observational drawing strategies (for example use contour drawing to depict the edges of form, use a viewfinder to frame a composition, use a magnifying glass to observe and draw close-ups, use gesture drawing to show movement, use scale and perspective in representing buildings).
Expresses their responses to real experiences in artworks (for example create an image of 'friends').

Creates images to communicate an idea, opinion or/and imagination (for example after viewing the work by Gauguin on the internet, use bright colours enclosed by lines in a landscape; after listening to lyrics of a song, create a 3-D work of an imaginary world).
Creates images or objects based on environment/social issues (for example make a poster to create awareness on global warming).

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply knowledge of art elements and principles to create two- and three-dimensional artworks using a range of materials, technologies and processes.

indicators.indicator: Drawing: Uses a variety of media and effects in drawing (for example make drawings of parts or all of a still life set up in the classroom using wet/dry media).
Collage: Uses collage to produce an image or represent an idea (for example use drawings, photographs and foil to make a collage of a flying machine).
Mixed media: Uses a combination of media to create artworks (for example use crayon and charcoal in a still life drawing to create form and depth).

Painting: Uses paints of different consistencies for creating artworks (for example make a cityscape painting to be used as a background in an animated short movie, using watercolour techniques such as wet on wet or salt resist).
Printmaking: Uses the techniques of simple printmaking (for example make a collograph that communicates a personal experience through the use of shape and analogous colour).

Models and crafts: Creates 3-D objects using a variety of available materials (for example use junk to create a robot or imaginary creature; explore ideas of exaggeration, distortion and expression, and find inventive ways to use cutting, scoring, shaping, and joining techniques).
Fabric and fibres: Uses selected skills and techniques to manipulate fabrics and thread (for example create a braided rug using recycled clothes).
Graphic design: Creates artworks to visually communicate information considering layout, imagery, lettering and audience (for example design a shopping mall banner).

Multimedia: Uses a computer software to create an artwork (for example make a high-contrast portrait with a computer software, using techniques such as blurring, cloning, cropping, distortion, layering, rotation and selection).

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Apply critical thinking strategies to analyse, reflect and respond to a variety of artwork and art experiences.

indicators.indicator: Interprets a variety of artworks and identifies the feelings, issues, themes and social concerns that they convey.
Describes how the elements and principles of design are used in their own and others' artwork using appropriate visual arts vocabulary.

Identifies and explains their strengths, interests and areas for improvement as creators, interpreters and viewers of art (for example provide constructive feedback as a critique of their own work and the works of others; identify strategies they used in planning, producing and criticising their own work and work of others).

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Experience and develop awareness of artworks from different periods of various time, places and cultures.

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes works of art and design from different periods of time, places and cultures (include: art and design from past and present and from local and global cultures).

Identifies, shares and discusses examples of art and design experienced first-hand in own community, the places and venues (for example galleries, mosques, public buildings, parks) where these experiences were gained.
Introspects and explains personal reasons for making art.
Recognises the importance of art in history and heritage.
Demonstrates an understanding of how to read and interpret signs, symbols and style in artworks (for example objects used as symbols in Sadako and the Thousand Paper Cranes by Eleanor Coerr).

Explains some of the ways in which artistic traditions in a variety of times and places have been maintained or adapted (for example migration or contact with other cultures have an influence on the forms and styles of art and architecture; artists incorporate the works of other artists or cultural traditions to make original artwork while also showing respect for others' cultural or intellectual property).

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Relate how personal lifestyle habits and heredity influence growth and development.

indicators.indicator: Describe how personal habits contribute towards growth and development.
Study and analyse experiences of individuals with regard to changes during puberty at different times with differing levels of intensity.
Discuss genetic factors that influence growth and development (genetic, inheritance, diet, exercise).
Identify different traits and whether the traits are inherited or acquired.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Analyse the cross over from childhood to adolescence and the changes associated with it.

indicators.indicator: Accept the changes in their body due to puberty as natural and a positive sign.
Understand emotions such as self-consciousness, shyness and frustration which are experienced by adolescents as natural and part of growing up.
Investigate the effect of peers, family and community on one’s identity and choices (for example, how peer group may influence the sense of dressing and behaviour).

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Identify and discuss issues relating to food and nutrition.

indicators.indicator: Understand the dietary requirements of the human body and how it differs in various age groups and professions.
Select food from the different levels of the food pyramid to design a healthy meal plan.

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Learn how to make healthy food choices in order to obtain and maintain healthy growth.

indicators.indicator: Understand the different type of food practices/habits and apply decision making skills when making food choices in order to ensure one’s health and wellbeing.
Compare product labels to establish nutritional value and suggest ways to improve the same by choosing healthier options.
Understand how advertising techniques are used to influence consumer decisions.
Select food from the different levels of the food pyramid to design a healthy and balanced nutritional meal plan based on nutritional content, value, calories and cost.

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Understand that healthy food habits reflect in a healthy body.

indicators.indicator: Explain the difference between a healthy and an unhealthy body.
Exhibit an understanding of malnutrition and its symptoms, causes and treatments.

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Identify and explore ways to keep food free from contamination.

indicators.indicator: Understand that contaminated food can result in food poisoning.
Explain the process of food contamination.
Take precautions and inculcate hygiene practices to ensure that food is not contaminated.
List causes and symptoms of common food-borne diseases.

Common diseases and disorders.outcomeStatement: Identify non-communicable diseases prevalent in the Maldives and take measures to prevent it.

indicators.indicator: Understand that early detection and treatment of diseases and health conditions impact one’s health.
Communicate health needs to trusted adults and professionals in order to assist in the prevention, early detection and treatment of health problems.
Summarize means of detecting and treating diseases and health conditions that are prevalent in adolescents in Maldives.
Connect causative factors, symptoms, treatment and preventive measures to their appropriate non-communicable diseases.

Alcohol, Drug and Substance Abuse.outcomeStatement: Describe the range of effects associated with using illicit drugs and intoxicating substances.

indicators.indicator: Understand the effect of alcohol, tobacco and drug use on the body systems.
Identify factors, such as peer pressure, that influences teen alcohol/tobacco/drug use
Read about school/local/state laws governing drug and alcohol use.

Alcohol, Drug and Substance Abuse.outcomeStatement: Determine effective strategies to prevent and stop using alcohol, tobacco and other drugs.

indicators.indicator: Learn to build resistance and refusal skills (learn to say ‘No’)
Use decision making strategies to make safe personal choices about the use of drugs such as alcohol, tobacco, cannabis etc.
Identify people and community resources that can provide support when dealing with situations involving substance abuse.
Summarize intervention strategies that assist family and friends to cope with the impact of substance abuse.

Personal and Sexual Identity.outcomeStatement: Examine ways to respond effectively to successes, challenges and failures that strengthen personal identities and sense of self.

indicators.indicator: Examine factors that support and contribute to personal achievement and success.
Propose strategies to respond positively to difficulties like positive self-talk, optimistic thinking etc.
Explain how meeting challenges and being successful at different activities can make you feel good about yourself and build confidence to try out new things.

Personal and Sexual Identity.outcomeStatement: Learns to accept their developing sexual identity as a part of puberty.

indicators.indicator: Understand that sexuality integrates many aspects of our lives like emotional, physical and interpersonal development of every person
Exhibit an understanding of their developing sexual identity and how new feelings like attraction are a part of growing up.

Relationships and Peer Pressure.outcomeStatement: Examines different types of relationships and develops skills to establish and manage a range of relationships that enhance their health and wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Incorporate values which help in resolving conflicts in relationships with family, kinfolk, friends, peers, teachers etc. (like negotiation, conflict resolution, problem solving)
Acknowledge the need to balance time they commit to maintaining different relationships (family/friends/social media).
Identifies positive relationships as those where moral support is offered and negative relationships as those where statements and attitudes are hostile.

Relationships and Peer Pressure.outcomeStatement: Discusses the skills required to deal with peer pressure and maintain positive relationships.

indicators.indicator: Explore how a peer group can influence an individual’s decision in a range of activities (consumption of alcohol or tobacco, dressing sense, values and attitudes)
Develop and practice ways of saying ‘No’ as the first step in learning to deal with peer pressures.

Mental Health and wellbeing.outcomeStatement: Understands that being healthy includes having both a healthy mind and a healthy body

indicators.indicator: Understand the difference between mental health and mental illness.
Practice behavior which contributes to his socially and mentally healthy persona like being confident, assertive and caring.
List ways to deal with stress, cope with change and handle it in a positive manner.
Analyse why some students may be cheerful while others may seem sad and how it could be related to their mental health.

Mental Health and wellbeing.outcomeStatement: Identifies sources which can help to stabilize the mind and ensure mental wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Learn to express ideas and opinions in ways that are sensitive and respectful in order to abstain from conflict and stress.
Learn to listen to others, make decisions, solve problems, exhibit leadership skills etc.
Identify people and community resources that can provide support when dealing with such situations.

Personal Hygiene and Dental Care.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate good health and hygiene habits in order to ensure personal health and wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Review personal hygiene practices (e.g. take a daily bath to keep skin, hair, ears, hands, and nails clean, brush teeth in the morning, after meals, and before bedtime, wear clean clothes).
Identify puberty specific hygiene practices regarding the growth of acne, blackheads, body odour, genital hygiene etc.
Understand the importance of washing hands and how it can prevent diseases and food contamination.

Personal Hygiene and Dental Care.outcomeStatement: Identify ways to prevent tooth decay and maintain good oral health for healthy living.

indicators.indicator: Understand the importance of taking good care of one’s teeth and gums in order to prevent tooth decay.
Identify ways to protect teeth.
Describe the relationship between sugar, bacteria and acid production.

Safety at home, school and community.outcomeStatement: Describe how effective decision making strategies can impact choices made by individuals or groups in regard to personal and collective safety.

indicators.indicator: Understand that it is everyone’s responsibility to ensure safety by paying attention to dangers.
Recognizes that accidents and unsafe situations can occur in school, at home or in unexpected places and circumstances.
Propose actions and protective behavior which can be implemented to protect their health and safety (wearing helmets, not taking risks in aquatic environment etc.)
Demonstrate behaviors and apply procedures that maximize their safety and that of others.

Safety at home, school and community.outcomeStatement: Describes how to react and follow safety rules in situations pertaining to road safety, fire safety, water safety and safety at public places.

indicators.indicator: Take preventive measures and follow safety instructions in situations pertaining to road safety, fire safety, water safety and safety at public places.
Understands the importance of internet safety and talks about risks associated with giving personal information through internet.

Emergency and First Aid.outcomeStatement: Explore emergency situations and how to carry out basic first aid skills.

indicators.indicator: Design an information card that lists what to do in emergency (e.g., tell an adult, phone no, ambulance).
Explains appropriate procedures for responding to personal, school and community emergencies.
Collaborating with peers to suggest strategies they could use in case of emergency.
Demonstrate basic first aid skills.
Creates a web of a network of people to go to when in emergency.

Health of Community and Environmental He.outcomeStatement: Describe how the global environment is being changed by human behavior and technological development in ways that effect health.

indicators.indicator: Understand how substances in the environment ultimately get into the human body through inhalation, absorption and ingestion.
Understand that it is everyone’s responsibility to ensure the protection of the environment and take measures to ensure the same.
Design a health promotion campaign aimed at improving environmental health in the light of it being changed by human behavior and technological development.

Health of Community and Environmental He.outcomeStatement: Recognizes the ill effects of improper waste disposal and how it can be managed to ensure cleanliness of the community.

indicators.indicator: Understand that improper waste disposal has a negative impact on our health and the environment.
Take preventive measures to ensure cleanliness of the community by exhibiting socially responsive behavior.

Health of Community and Environmental He.outcomeStatement: Recognizes the importance of water safety in the community and precautions against possible pollutants.

indicators.indicator: Understand that water can be polluted due to various reasons (like oil spills, chemical wastes etc.) and that polluted water is harmful for health.
Take preventive measures by exhibiting socially responsive behavior to ensure prevention of water pollution.

Fundamental Movement Skills.outcomeStatement: Perform a broad range of fundamental movement skills and demonstrate how the body can move in relation to other objects and people.

indicators.indicator: Participate in sustained moderate physical activity with appropriate warm up and cool-down activities to the best of their ability for twenty minutes every day.
Perform locomotor and non-locomotor skills in a coordinated manner with agility and strength in order to incorporate movement concepts.
Practice locomotor fundamental skills like walking, running, jumping, sliding, hopping, leaping, skipping and galloping.
Practice non-locomotor skills like bending, twisting, turning, balancing, pushing, pulling, stretching and rocking.
Practice manipulative skills like throwing, catching, dribbling, rolling, trapping, punting, kicking, volleying.

Understand movement concepts and display spatial awareness (space, direction, planes), body awareness (shapes, balance, flight, transfer of body weight), quality of movement (time, speed, force, flow) with body parts, objects and/or people.

Educational Gymnastics and Athletics.outcomeStatement: Seeks to provide an understanding and appreciation of expressive movement and sequencing in order to emphasize affective, cognitive and psychomotor development.

indicators.indicator: Perform simple individual sequences incorporating movement concepts and various forms of locomotion.
Explore different inverted static balances (e.g. shoulder stand, tripod stand, hand stand), co-operative balances, counterbalance, showing space and body awareness, and relationships.
Practice gymnastic actions like rolls (different directions, levels and ways to get in and out of rolls), different jumps, balances, shapes etc.

Educational Gymnastics and Athletics.outcomeStatement: Acquire the knowledge and skills to perform fundamental athletic movements and games.

indicators.indicator: Perform fundamental athletic movements pertaining to running (like sprints, distance running, relay etc), jumping and throwing (overarm, underarm, chest throw)
Explore and practice track events like sprints, relays, hurdles and distance running.
Explore and practice field events like long jump, high jump, triple jump etc.
Explore a range of territorial games like basketball, handball, soccer etc.
Explore and practice Net/Wall games like badminton, tennis, volleyball etc.
Explore and practice striking and fielding games like cricket, softball etc.

Swimming.outcomeStatement: Perform basic swimming strokes, underwater skill and survival techniques.

indicators.indicator: Understand the nuances of pool and water safety.
Demonstrate confidence in water without buoyancy aids.
Practice floating skills and at least two recognized strokes.
Understand water safety, e.g. personal water safety at home pools, beaches and river mouths.
Understand basic water safety (causes of drowning accidents, dangers of open water, prevention of accidents)
Practise life-saving techniques (motionless floating variations, treading water in different ways e.g. life-saving kick, straddle jump, standing dive, treading water)

Health and Skill related fitness.outcomeStatement: Understands the components of health-related fitness and assess their level (cardiorespiratory endurance, muscular strength, flexibility) and monitor changes in their physical fitness over time.

indicators.indicator: Recognizes the difference between health-related components of personal fitness (cardio-respiratory endurance, flexibility etc.) and skill-related components (i.e balance, agility, power, time, speed, coordination) and devise ways to enhance both.
Points out benefits of physical activity for the heart, lungs, and muscles (for example understands skipping contributes to the prevention of heart disease).
Compare how they feel, before, during and after a fitness task.
Compare their personal results and physical activity participation over a period of time such as the beginning, middle and end of academic year.
Ensure safety practices during physical activity.

Health and Skill related fitness.outcomeStatement: Participate in physical activities designed to develop particular aspects of health-related and skill-related fitness and discuss the impact regular participation in these activities can have on health and wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Describing and demonstrating safe stretching activities which maintain and develop flexibility.
Identifying the activities they participate in regularly that maintain and improve cardiovascular endurance.

Use of Calculator.outcomeStatement: Understand how to use a calculator efficiently.

indicators.indicator: Understand basics of a calculator (on/off, operations, fractions, brackets, squares).

indicators.indicator: Explains the role of the British and Dutch in the Maldives
