indicators.indicator: Describes independent movements and the role of significant leaders of the nationalist movements

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of World War II and the situation of Maldives during World War II

indicators.indicator: Describes World War II

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the Addu-Huvadhoo Rebellion

indicators.indicator: Explains the Addu-Huvadhoo Rebellion and its significance

Eras of Change and Conflict.outcomeStatement: Explore and understand the November 3rd circumstances and events

indicators.indicator: Describes the November 3rd circumstances and events

Belief Systems.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of Islamic civilisations in South Asia and South East Asia and the rapid growth of Islam in those regions

indicators.indicator: Describes Islamic civilisations in South Asia and South East Asia

Belief Systems.outcomeStatement: Explore the legends and stories behind the pre-Islamic era of Maldives and the events that contributed to Maldives becoming an Islamic nation

indicators.indicator: Describes the legends and stories behind the pre-Islamic era of Maldives

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the transition from hunter gatherer to food producing economy and significant creations that people in the past have built

indicators.indicator: Explains the transition and change from the hunter gatherer economy to food producing economy

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Explore and demonstrate understanding about stories behind the early settlement in Male' and the significance of Male' during different time periods of history

indicators.indicator: Explains the stories behind the early settlement in Male'

Early People and Ancient Stories.outcomeStatement: Find out and depict understanding about stories/events that have had great impacts on groups of people in the local communities

indicators.indicator: Identifies and investigates stories/events related to leprosy and cholera and their impact on local communities

Historical Inquiry Skills.outcomeStatement: Apply skills used in the historical inquiry and analysis

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates skills used in the historical inquiry and analysis

Change Over Time.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of urbanisation and the formation of towns and cities

indicators.indicator: Explains urbanisation and the formation of towns and cities

Change Over Time.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding about the sultanate regime in the Maldives and the contributions of significant kings and queens

indicators.indicator: Describes the sultanate regime in the Maldives

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Apply map reading skills to compare and contrast different forms of maps and interpret graphs

indicators.indicator: Compares and contrasts different forms of maps and map projections

The World in Spatial Terms.outcomeStatement: Explore traditional methods of travelling by sea, significant stars used by local travelers and specific tools in travelling by sea

indicators.indicator: Describes traditional methods of travelling by sea and significant stars used by local travelers

Human Systems and Interactions.outcomeStatement: Explore types of agriculture, improved technology used in agriculture and its environmental impacts

indicators.indicator: Identifies types of agriculture

Human Systems and Interactions.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of hierarchy of settlements and problems associated with urbanisation

indicators.indicator: Explains the hierarchy of settlements and patterns in urban structures

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness of impacts and loss due to natural hazards

indicators.indicator: Describes the impacts and loss due to natural hazards

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Recognize geographical statistics of Maldives islands and Island and atoll profiles

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes geographical statistics of Maldives islands

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of climatic patterns, the interrelated processes that produce them and their impact on environments

indicators.indicator: Describes climatic patterns and the interrelated processes that produce them

Physical Environments.outcomeStatement: Explore water bodies of the world, its formation and process

indicators.indicator: Describes water bodies of the world, their formation and process

Geography, A Sense of the World.outcomeStatement: Explore geology geography and everyday life

indicators.indicator: Describes how geology geography relates to everyday life

Introduction to Travel and Tourism.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of classifications of tourists and tourism services

indicators.indicator: Identifies and describes classifications of tourists

Features of Worldwide Destinations.outcomeStatement: Explore features of location and specific attractions, and the influence of physical features on tourism development

indicators.indicator: Describes features of location and specific attractions to a location

Impacts of Tourism.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of economic impacts of tourism and eco-friendly approaches

indicators.indicator: Describes economic impacts of tourism

Tourism in the Maldives.outcomeStatement: Explore changing trends in domestic tourism and improved facilities in local tourism

indicators.indicator: Describes changing trends in domestic tourism

Heritage.outcomeStatement: Identify significant possible ways to sustain the national heritages

indicators.indicator: Explores and discusses significant possible ways to sustain the national heritages

Heritage.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of historical monuments, buildings and places considered to be as heritage that has been damaged in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Identifies historical monuments, buildings and places considered to be as heritage that has been damaged in the Maldives

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore the variety of ways in which culture is created and expressed through food

indicators.indicator: Describes the variety of ways in which culture is created and expressed through food

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore roles, features and cultural practices over a time of the past and how cultural practices can change meaning overtime

indicators.indicator: Describes roles, features and cultural practices over a time of the past

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore cultural identity expressed through literature

indicators.indicator: Identifies and analyses cultural identity expressed through literature

Identity and Culture.outcomeStatement: Explore culture attributes in building identity factors

indicators.indicator: Identifies culture attributes in building identity factors

Research.outcomeStatement: Locate sources, organize and select information

indicators.indicator: Locates sources and organises and selects information for an investigation

Planning.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate skills needed for an investigation

indicators.indicator: Demonstrates skills needed to plan for an investigation

Processing and Translating.outcomeStatement: Apply skills relevant for processing and translating information and develop critical thinking

indicators.indicator: Applies skills relevant for processing and translating information

Applying and Communicating.outcomeStatement: Apply, communicate findings by reflecting on the process and share information with an audience

indicators.indicator: Applies and communicates findings by reflecting on the process

Globalisation.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the characteristics of globalisation

indicators.indicator: Identifies the characteristics of globalisation

Globalisation.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the effects of globalisation at local, national and global scale

indicators.indicator: Describes the effects of globalisation at local, national and global scale

Regional, Voluntary and Global Organisat.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate awareness of significant NGOs in the global community and their contributions

indicators.indicator: Identifies significant NGOs in the global community and their mission and contributions across the nations

International Relations.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the role of global organizations for international peace keeping and humanitarian activities

indicators.indicator: Describes the role of some of the global organizations whose mandate is to work for international peace keeping and promotes humanitarian activities

Government Systems.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of characteristics and role of good governance and its relationship with democracy

indicators.indicator: Identifies characteristics of good governance and the role of good governance

Political Parties.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the history of political parties in the Maldives and the role of political parties

indicators.indicator: Describes the history of political parties in the Maldives

Structure and Function of the Government.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the structure and function of the three branches of government in Maldives

indicators.indicator: Describes the structure of the government and function of the government

Law and Order.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of law and order and the process of law making and the function of the law making bodies

indicators.indicator: Describes law and order and the process of law making

Law and Order.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the hierarchy and the role of the judicial system in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Describes the hierarchy of the judicial system in the Maldives

Rights, Responsibilities and Citizenship.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the legal status of citizens and rights, duties and responsibilities in the Maldives

indicators.indicator: Explains the legal status of 'citizen' in the Maldives

Rights, Responsibilities and Citizenship.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate understanding of the importance of active and informed citizenship and major conventions signed by Maldives

indicators.indicator: Explains the importance of active and informed citizenship

Rights, Responsibilities and Citizenship.outcomeStatement: Explore constitutional articles about national symbols and the interrelationship between national symbols and citizenship

indicators.indicator: Identifies constitutional articles about national symbols

Social Systems and Social Virtues.outcomeStatement: Explore ways in which individuals, groups and governments can contribute to the preservation of human rights

indicators.indicator: Describes ways in which individuals, groups and governments can contribute to the preservation of human rights

Social Systems and Social Virtues.outcomeStatement: Explore the ways for peace building and conflict resolution

indicators.indicator: Explores the ways for peace building and conflict resolution

Social Systems and Social Virtues.outcomeStatement: Create awareness about perseverance, hard work and contribution made by significant global leaders and youth leaders

indicators.indicator: Identifies significant global leaders and youth leaders and their contributions

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply the elements of drama in various genres and styles of theatre.

indicators.indicator: Performs different styles of drama using a series of props, sets, costumes and make-up.
Applies characterisations, conflicts motivation in innovative ways and different settings in the drama.
Integrates and identifies the reasons for using the 5 W's (who, what, where, when, why).
Sets and uses the stage according to the plan (blocking concepts) he/she makes.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply process and skills in acting and directing to perform in class presentations.

indicators.indicator: Conveys mood and characterisation by using effective voice modulation techniques and proper body language.
Portrays character using appropriate acting skills such as proper facial expressions, voice modulations, gestures, etc. and special effects such as make-up, wigs and costumes.
Plays a character within a production with confidence, in a meaningful way by memorising lines and using complex stage directions.
Incorporates props, sets, make-up, and costumes, in a production to portray characters and discuss about the lighting that could have been used.
Develops and uses tension and suspense in a drama using characters and actions.
Comes up with innovative ways of staging in classroom presentations (plays/drama).
Improvises a given story in groups to develop characters, actions and environments for dramatisation of the story.
Uses technological means and improvises sequentially, freely and immediately with confidence and imagination.
Works in groups to enhance creative ideas in planning and implementing a theatrical production.

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Reflect, describe, analyse and communicate feelings, ideas and understanding in response to their own theatrical work and the work of others.

indicators.indicator: Discusses performance and presentation experiences, to identify what has been effective and what needs to be developed, paying attention to what worked well, and why.
Expresses their own views on drama elements and issues, and suggest ways of improving them.
Demonstrates a good knowledge of drama vocabulary.
Evaluates and gives constructive feedback on a drama presentation.

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Discuss the understanding of a variety of drama and theatre forms, traditions, and styles from the past and present, and their socio-cultural and historical contexts.

indicators.indicator: Compares varying cultural perspectives and how they are expressed through drama.
Identifies, understands and appreciates some of the varied roles of theatre in different communities and cultures.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Create and improvise vocal melodies and vocal accompaniments using variety of vocal techniques.

indicators.indicator: Sings various songs in major and minor scales.
Identifies intervals by ear (unison, major third, minor third, perfect fifth and octave).
Identifies chord progressions. For example, (I, IV, V) (I, VI, IV, V).
Applies different voice techniques (legato, staccato, vibrato).
Performs a three parts vocal composition in groups.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Perform and understand the use of scale in instruments and play traditional beats.

indicators.indicator: Performs a repertoire of instrumental literature with expression and technical accuracy on an instrument.
Performs at least three major scales and three minor scales as well as a chromatic scale with an instrument of their choice (one octave). (Recommended: recorder, keyboard, guitar)
Improvises rhythmic patterns with boduberu main beats.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Compose and arrange music within guidelines specified by the teacher.

indicators.indicator: Identifies relative minor scales and constructs melodic minor and harmonic minor scales.
Composes and arranges a four parts (bass, tenor, alto and soprano) vocal composition within a specified guideline.

Creates different rhythmic patterns using whole note, half notes, dotted quarter notes, quarter notes, quarter note triplets, dotted eighth notes, eight-note triplets, eighth notes, sixteenth notes and rests.

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Apply critical thinking strategies to analyse, reflect and respond to a variety of music and musical experiences.

indicators.indicator: Expresses analytical and/or personal responses to musical performances.

Uses musical terminology to analyse ways in which the elements are used in the music that they perform, listen to and create (for example listen to a folk song or a current popular song and describe how the use of various elements affect their response to the music).

Identifies and gives examples of their strengths and areas of improvement as composers, musical performers, interpreters, and audience members (for example keep a practice journal, record and analyse their performances throughout the term).

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Explore and list factors influencing creation of music and performance, and investigate music from the past and different cultures.

indicators.indicator: Compares music from the past and present.

Analyses some of the social and economic factors that affect the creation of music (for example historical events that inspired the composition of nationalistic music; the social and/or cultural origins of patriotic/national songs, love songs; the economic purposes for commercial music played in dramas/plays/movies).
Uses traditional and digital tools to compose, arrange music and produce musical sounds.
Explores the influence of different cultures on Maldivian music.
Performs music from different cultures with expression.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Use the elements of art in artworks to communicate ideas, messages and understanding for a specific audience and purpose.

indicators.indicator: Line: Uses directional lines to create eye movement to a focal point.
Line: Uses one- and two-point perspectives to create the illusion of depth.
Line: Makes contour drawings of human figures.
Shape and form: Creates a non-objective artwork using geometric and/or organic shapes or forms.
Shape and form: Creates complex 3-dimensional constructions (both geometric and organic).
Colour: Identifies and creates analogous colours.
Colour: Creates transparent effect using watercolours or tissue paper découpage.
Colour: Creates multimedia artworks with some understanding of different colour models such as RGB and CMYK, and web safe colours.
Texture: Creates a variety of visual and actual textures using a variety of tools, materials and techniques.
Value: Uses gradation of value to create illusion of depth with different shading techniques (for example cross hatching).
Space: Uses one-point and two-point perspectives in an image to create illusory space.

Space: Uses alternative systems for representing space (for example layered images, disproportionately small images of people within a vast landscape in Chinese art to show how diminutive humans are as compared to nature; images seen from several points of view simultaneously in Egyptian and cubist paintings).

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate an understanding of composition using multiple principles of design and other layout considerations such as compositional triangles to create narrative artworks and/or artworks on a theme or topic.

indicators.indicator: Creates regular and random patterns by repeating a motif (for example look at the repeated motif of 'soup can' by Andy Warhol and create a similar pattern based on an everyday item).
Creates an artwork that demonstrates the use of actual action/movement (for example a stop-motion animation using sequential images in which the character or object moves in relation to the frame).
Uses improbable scale for imaginary settings and creatures (for example caricature, cartooning).
Demonstrates the use of asymmetrical balance in an artwork (for example 'The Flower Carrier' by Diego Rivera, 'The Wave' by Hokusai).
Creates artwork to show contrast (for example light shades next to dark shades, soft lines next to bold lines).
Applies emphasis (creates a particular area of focus) in an artwork.

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Create images and objects based on ideas, feelings, personal experiences, imagination, observation and a study of artists' works.

indicators.indicator: Uses a visual journal to document the process of the development of ideas (for example keep a visual diary/portfolio that describes the process of achieving a final design and the evaluation of the final piece).

Creates images based on observational drawing (for example after studying the lines, forms and textures of a plant or flower, make observational line drawings of the plant's botanical features from the 'point of view' of an ant or bumblebee).
Expresses their responses to real experiences in visual arts work (for example investigate the topic 'My Environment' through a series of drawings to express a personal viewpoint on pollution).

Creates images to communicate an idea or/and imagination (for example explore Pop Art of the 1960s and look at Andy Warhol's silkscreen images of actors and singers and apply these ideas to create a modern 'star').

Creates images or objects based on the themes/styles or content of an artist (for example adapt a realistic drawing of your favourite food item to create a sculpture in the style of Claes Oldenburg the Pop artist).

Creating and Presenting.outcomeStatement: Apply knowledge of art elements and principles to create two- and three-dimensional artworks using a wide range of art materials, tools, technologies and processes.

indicators.indicator: Drawing: Creates drawing in different media and explores their effects (for example develop a series of line drawings in wet and dry media to tell a story based on a family or peer group experience).
Collage: Uses a collage to produce an image or represent an idea (for example gather images from magazines for a montage based on landscapes).
Mixed media: Combines different painting and drawing media to create artworks (for example work back into a tonal collage with black and white inks to create contrast).
Painting: Applies selected painting techniques (for example use thick, impasto paint and gesso to create interesting textures in a work).

Printmaking: Uses selected techniques to transfer an image to another surface (for example make a 'block', rolling ink evenly over the surface and printing onto another surface such as paper or fabric).
Models and crafts: Creates 3-D objects from a variety of available materials (for example use wire, fabric and recycled materials to create an imaginary animal/object).
Fabric and fibres: Uses selected skills and techniques to manipulate fabrics and thread (for example make a puppet using simple and decorative stitches).

Graphic design: Creates artworks to visually communicate information considering layout, imagery, lettering and audience (for example design a CD cover, considering image, lettering and the effect of different colour schemes).

Multimedia: Uses digital media and software programs as a tool to create artworks (for example use appropriate software to manipulate a digital image to explore the different effects which can be developed to create a specific mood).

Reflecting, Responding and Analysing.outcomeStatement: Apply critical thinking strategies to analyse, reflect and respond to a variety of artwork and art experiences.

indicators.indicator: Interprets a variety of artworks and identifies the feelings, issues, themes and social concerns that they convey.
Analyses how the elements and principles of design are used in their own and others' artwork using appropriate visual arts vocabulary.

Identifies and evaluates their strengths, interests, and areas of improvement as creators, interpreters and viewers of art (for example organise and participate in a non-competitive art show that documents the stages of the artistic process from artists' statements, concept drawings and photos of works in progress to the final artworks; use feedback to evaluate the effectiveness of their own artworks).

Exploring History and Cultural Context.outcomeStatement: Experience and develop awareness about artworks from different periods of time, places and cultures.

indicators.indicator: Compares two or more works of art and design from different periods of time, places and cultures (include: art and design from past and present and from local and global cultures).

Identifies, shares, and discusses examples of art and design experienced first-hand in own community, places and venues (for example galleries, mosques, public buildings, parks) where these experiences occurred.
Demonstrates an understanding of the multiple roles and purposes of art and design in society (for example personal fulfilment, cultural expressions and commercial persuasion).
Recognises the importance of art in history and heritage.
Demonstrates an understanding of how to read and interpret signs, symbols and styles in artworks (for example use of visual metaphor in paintings; use of traditional symbols in contemporary art).
Explains how social, cultural and economic factors affect the creation of artists or designers.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Compare and contrast the impact of family history and personal health practices on personal growth and development.

indicators.indicator: Describe how family history and personal health practices contribute towards growth and development.
Understand the reproductive system of their sex.
Exhibit an understanding of the menstrual cycle and sperm production.

Healthy Body Awareness.outcomeStatement: Investigate personal, physical and social changes that occur as they grow older and propose and apply practical strategies to manage these.

indicators.indicator: Understand his/her sexual identity and how it is a part of growth and development.

Explore and understand how roles and responsibilities change (such as travelling to and from school, helping with household chores and the responsibility of caring for younger siblings), and apply strategies to manage these responsibilities along with their other commitments.
Respect and accept change in others (such as friends, interests, appearance and behaviour), and evaluate how these changes make them feel.
Recognise the dynamic nature of feelings towards others, and discuss how the way they feel may change over time.

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Identify and discuss issues relating to food and nutrition.

indicators.indicator: Describe balanced diet and understand the importance of eating a variety of foods in right proportions.
Understand the difference between natural foods and genetically modified foods and the harm it can cause.

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Learn how to make healthy food choices in order to obtain and maintain healthy growth.

indicators.indicator: Identify opportunities to make personal choices to enhance their own health, such as food choices when out with friends.

Research and compare the cost of fresh foods and fast foods, their nutritional value, and monitor the sale of fresh food and fast food items in outlets in the local area to draw conclusions about influences on dietary choices.
Explore a range of health decisions and analyse the factors that influence the choices they make (role of media and advertising, how accessibility and cost may influence their food choices)

Nutrition and Eating Disorders.outcomeStatement: Understand that healthy food habits reflect in a healthy body.

indicators.indicator: Understand the relationship between food consumption, weight and exercise.
Exhibit an understanding of the names, symptoms and causes of eating disorders.

Diseases.outcomeStatement: Identify and explore ways to keep food free from contamination.

indicators.indicator: Take measures against the prevention of food spoilage
Take measures against the prevention of food-borne diseases.

Diseases.outcomeStatement: Identify communicable diseases prevalent in the Maldives and take measures to prevent it.

indicators.indicator: Define communicable diseases and explain the importance of early detection and treatment of diseases.
Connect causative factors, symptoms, treatment and preventive measures to their appropriate communicable disease.
Differentiate between communicable and non-communicable diseases.
Compare and contrast situations that require support from trusted adults or health individuals.

Alcohol, Drug and Substance Abuse.outcomeStatement: Describe the range of effects associated with using illicit drugs and intoxicating substances.

indicators.indicator: Analyse varying effects of the use, misuse and abuse of over the counter, prescription and illegal drugs have on different individuals.
Examine how drugs/alcohol effect rational decisions and may lead to illness, injury, disease and risky health behaviors.
Identify legal consequences of the use, sale and possession of illegal substances.

Alcohol, Drug and Substance Abuse.outcomeStatement: Determine effective strategies to prevent and stop using alcohol, tobacco and other drugs.

indicators.indicator: Compare and contrast theories about addiction and dependency and provide recommendations that support a drug free life.
Identify and analyse influences from peers and media promotions that impact choices regarding unhealthy behavior like smoking, drinking, dieting etc.
Explain how preoccupation with body image or athletic performance can contribute to substance abuse (misuse of supplements, vitamins, diet pills, steroids).
Determine situations where use of alcohol and drugs influence decision making and can place one at risk.
Summarize intervention strategies that assist family and friends to cope with the impact of substance abuse.

Personal and Sexual Identity.outcomeStatement: Shows positive self-esteem, builds character and learns to respect and accept diversity thereby strengthening personal identity.

indicators.indicator: Understand the need to build self-confidence and positive self-esteem.
Learn to respect and accept individuals and their individual differences and similarities regardless of their gender, physical appearance, disability, ethnicity, socio-economic background, religion.
Investigate groups and communities to which they belong and their influence on their identity.
Identify ways of managing negative emotions in positive ways.
Identify situations where there is need to stand up to pressure.

Personal and Sexual Identity.outcomeStatement: Learns to accept their developing sexual identity as a part of puberty.

indicators.indicator: Understand that sexuality integrates many aspects of our lives like emotional, physical and interpersonal development of every person
Discuss the possible physical, social and emotional impact of adolescent decisions regarding sexual behavior.
Discusses the impact of peer influence on their developing sexual identity.

Relationship and Peer Pressure.outcomeStatement: Examines the changing nature of relationships and develops skills to manage a range of relationships that enhance their health and wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Explore the characteristics of positive and respectful relationships.
Discuss actions that can be taken if a relationship is not respectful.
Understand how following online social protocols helps to enhance their relationships with others (like not sharing passwords, asking for permission to post a photo).
Investigate the changing nature of their family relationships and recognize why they may be experiencing increased tensions with parents.
Recognize and respect the dynamic nature of feelings towards others and how relationships may change over time.

Relationship and Peer Pressure.outcomeStatement: Discusses the skills required to deal with peer pressure and maintain positive relationships.

indicators.indicator: Identify and discuss situations where emotional responses can be unpredictable and recognize that there is more than one way of looking at things (sharing information about a friend can also be seen as gossiping)
Learn to distinguish between positive and negative peer influence.
Discuss ways by which peer pressure can be resisted.

Mental Health and wellbeing.outcomeStatement: Evaluate the influence of emotional responses and how it affects mental wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Analyse how emotions influence decision making and may result in stress (losing your temper with a friend over a trivial mistake and how it can harm your relationship) affecting your mental wellbeing.
Identify common mental illnesses like depression, anxiety and panic disorders and determine methods to detect and treat them.
Determine the effects that mental illnesses have on the overall wellbeing of people who are afflicted with them.

Mental Health and wellbeing.outcomeStatement: Identifies sources which can help to stabilize the mind and ensure mental wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Explore a range of scenarios young people may encounter in relation to mental health and wellbeing, and discuss realistic options and strategies for dealing with these situations.
Compare and contrast stress management strategies in order to ensure mental wellbeing.
Identify people and community resources that can provide support when dealing with such situations.

Personal Hygiene and Dental Care.outcomeStatement: Demonstrate the ability to make informed decisions relating to personal hygiene.

indicators.indicator: Establish daily habits for caring for their bodies in order to improve health and prevent illnesses.
List effective ways of protecting oneself by following good personal hygiene (like not coughing on others, throwing away items which have germs etc.)
Identify cultural aspects of cleanliness and the importance of observing culture-led cleanliness.
Pay attention to not only keeping themselves clean but also their home.

Personal Hygiene and Dental Care.outcomeStatement: Appreciate and understand the importance of oral health for healthy living.

indicators.indicator: Identify ways to protect teeth and alter his/her lifestyle to bring about the change.
Describe dental terms like plaque, cavity etc.
Understand that he/she should brush twice daily with a Fluoride toothpaste since it helps prevent cavities by strengthening and protecting tooth enamel.
Identify nutritious foods which help in keeping their teeth and body healthy.

Safety at home, school and community.outcomeStatement: Analyse and predict factors that may require the use of effective decision making strategies to ensure personal and collective safety.

indicators.indicator: Predict potentially dangerous situations and make strategies and decisions to prevent any harm.
Refrain from participating in activities that may cause harm to any individual, group or a thing and devise ways to prevent any sort of damage.
Propose actions and protective behavior which can be implemented to protect their health and safety (wearing helmets, not taking risks in aquatic environment etc.)

Safety at home, school and community.outcomeStatement: Describes how to react and follow safety rules in situations pertaining to road safety, fire safety, water safety, internet safety and safety at public places.

indicators.indicator: Take preventive measures and follow safety instructions in situations pertaining to road safety, fire safety, water safety and safety at public places.
Recognizes instances of bullying and brainstorm ideas of what to do when met with a bully.
Understands the importance of internet safety and discuss situations which may make them vulnerable online (cyber bullying etc.)

Emergency and First Aid.outcomeStatement: Explore emergency situations and how to deal with them with basic First Aid skills.

indicators.indicator: Explains appropriate procedures for responding to personal, school and community emergencies.
Collaborating with peers to suggest strategies they could use in case of emergency.
Demonstrate basic first aid skills.
Demonstrate and describe first aid procedures which would include the care of head injuries, wound assessment, treatment of bleeding wounds, burns, fractures etc.

Environmental Health.outcomeStatement: Describe how the global environment is being changed by human behavior and technological development in ways that effect health.

indicators.indicator: Investigate the causes and effects of over consumption and pollution of natural resources and how to take better care of our air, land and water to ensure a healthy environment.
Discusses how technology can influence the environment and thereby improve or damage health.
Talks about the impact on people’s health due to the changes in ozone layer.
Describes how chemical use can change the ecosystem and influence health.

Environmental Health.outcomeStatement: Recognizes the ill effects of air pollution and how it can be managed to ensure a healthy community.

indicators.indicator: Understand that air can be polluted due to various reasons (like smoke, carbon monoxide, chemical fumes etc.) and that polluted air is harmful for health.
Take preventive measures by exhibiting socially responsive behavior to ensure prevention of air pollution.

Environmental Health.outcomeStatement: Recognizes how the environment can impact human health by natural disasters and why it is important to maintain a healthy ecosystem.

indicators.indicator: Understand that natural disasters occur because of geological factors and not caused directly by human activity.
Explain the occurrence of natural disasters like flood, drought, earthquake, volcanic eruption, tsunami etc.
Make a community risk and resource map which shows what risks exist in your community and what resources you can use to protect yourself.

Fundamental Movement Skills.outcomeStatement: Perform a broad range of fundamental movement skills and demonstrate how the body can move in relation to other objects and people.

indicators.indicator: Participate in sustained moderate physical activity with appropriate warm up and cool-down activities to the best of their ability for twenty minutes every day.

Perform a variety of locomotor movements, with and without equipment, while responding to a variety of external stimuli (dodge and fake in response to others, accelerate before taking off for a high/long jump)

Understand movement concepts and display spatial awareness (space, direction, planes), body awareness (shapes, balance, flight, transfer of body weight), quality of movement (time, speed, force, flow) with body parts, objects and/or people
Demonstrate an understanding of the phases of movement (i.e. preparation, execution, follow through)

Apply the understanding of movement to the refinement of skills as they participate in a variety of physical activities (bending knees to get ready to jump, thrust arms up for extra force while jumping, hold a controlled body position in flight, bend knees and put arms out for a stable landing)
Compare and contrast how various movement skills are affected by a change in force and motion (i.e. weight transfer, power, speed and agility)

Explain and perform movement skills that combine mechanically correct movement in smooth flowing sequences in isolated settings (i.e. skill practice) and applied settings (i.e. games, sports and recreational activities)
Detect, analyse and correct errors to refine movement skills.

Educational Gymnastics and Athletics.outcomeStatement: Seeks to provide an understanding and appreciation of expressive movement and sequencing in order to emphasize affective, cognitive and psychomotor development.

indicators.indicator: Perform an extended individual sequence of movements and balances with good form, and rhythm incorporating various gymnastic actions.
Change weight bearing to different parts of the body, moving towards mini cartwheel.
Practice and refine gymnastic actions which deal with transfer of body weight like wheeling actions, kipping actions etc.
Demonstrate control when transitioning between twists, rotations and leaps in an aerobic routine.

Educational Gymnastics and Athletics.outcomeStatement: Acquire the knowledge and skills to perform fundamental athletic movements.

indicators.indicator: Demonstrate an understanding of the components of a range of physical activities (movement skills, game structure, rules and guidelines, conventions of fair play and etiquette) and apply this understanding as they participate in a variety of physical activities in indoor and outdoor environments.
Perform fundamental athletic movements pertaining to running (like sprints, distance running, relay etc), jumping and throwing (overarm, underarm, chest throw)
Learn skills like passing, receiving, marking, intercepting, tackling and challenging in athletic games.
Learn how to react and respond to opponent’s play and identify situational problems pertinent to the game.
Devise strategies and refine decision making skills in order to win the game.
Practice and refine a range of territorial, Net/Wall and striking and fielding games.

Swimming.outcomeStatement: Perform basic swimming strokes, underwater skill and survival techniques.

indicators.indicator: Learn the codes of hygiene, safety and courtesy while using swimming pools.
Practice and refine recognized strokes like breaststroke, front crawl, backstroke and butterfly confidently and with ease.
Learn to jump and dive into the water in different ways.
Gain insight into water safety and life-saving techniques

Experience enjoyment during participation in “challenging” water activities with objects (diving through hoop, carrying ball/hoop while swimming, relays with balls, staying on top of a tube, pull a partner on tube while swimming, diving/swimming through hoops)

Health and Skill related fitness.outcomeStatement: Understands the components of health-related fitness and assess their level (cardiorespiratory endurance, muscular strength, flexibility) and monitor changes in their physical fitness over time.

indicators.indicator: Develop and implement plans of action based on both their assessments of their health related components of fitness and their interest in order to achieve personal fitness goals (like improving cardiorespiratory fitness by doing activities that increase the heart rate in order to be able to walk or run faster)

Understand how the FITT principle affects development of components of health-related fitness (for example understands the importance of playing active games frequently, with high intensity, contributes to heart health, as compared to sedentary activities such as playing video games for a long period of time).

Develop, implement and revise a personal plan to meet short term health related fitness goal based on the F.I.T.T principle (by using personal assessment information, appropriate training principles, identifying challenges and sources of support, monitoring progress etc.)

Understands how the components of health-related fitness and improved performance in a variety of activities are related (for example understands that a healthy level of muscular endurance would enable an individual to swim or bicycle for a long period of time).

Health and Skill related fitness.outcomeStatement: Participate in physical activities designed to develop particular aspects of health-related and skill-related fitness and discuss the impact regular participation in these activities can have on health and wellbeing.

indicators.indicator: Matching health-related fitness components with an activity that develops them such as cardiovascular (aerobic) fitness and shuttle running, sit ups and core strength, stretching and flexibility.
Constructing a circuit of appropriate activities for developing health-related fitness components of strength, cardiovascular endurance, muscular endurance and flexibility.
Measuring heart rate and breathing rate to evaluate the intensity of a range of activities, and to determine the activities best suited to improving cardiovascular endurance.
